⭐These deposits are considered by most lenders as the customers primary income⭐
General Deposits – All deposits, expect ATM / P2P / Internal Transfer / Refunds / Credits / Loan Advances
ATM Deposits – Cash / check deposits made at an ATM
P2P Credits – Credits issued via Zelle, Venmo, etc.
5. Other Deposits
These deposits are NOT considered by most lenders as income. They are broken into 3 categories:
Internal Transfers – Credits resulting from movement of funds across multiple accounts owned by the customer
Refunds / Credits – Credits issued which can be classified as one-time payments such as Tax Refunds or any gov’t issued refunds
Loan Advances – Credits received resulting from loan payments from financial institutions
6. Insights
Days Negative – # of days the account was in a negative position during a specific period.
Overdraft / NSF Fees – # of times a fee was issued due to Overdraft and/or NSF during a specific period.
Withdrawals – total debits during a specific period
Net - Total Deposits minus Withdrawals
Average Balance – Average balance maintained in the account during a specific period. Calculated by averaging the Total Deposits minus the average of Withdrawals.
Status Definitions
"Status" refers to the point in the TurboPass invitation process where the customer is / last left off.
* - This indicates that there are other technical statuses that have a similar meaning. To determine your specific technical status, you can check the "History" section under the "Actions" column on the dashboard.
User Roles / Permissions
Is Manager? - Allows the user to have full admin access to the account like creating users, changing the subscription and more.
Can View Reports? - Considerably one of the most important permissions. This permission allows the user to view customer's TurboPass reports sent by that user.
List all TurboPasses - Within the Organization permission enables the new user to view ALL TurboPass reports and invitations sent by other users within the organization.
Enable MFA?  - Requires a verification code to be sent to the TurboPass users email before each log in.
Send User Notification?  - Allows the user to send customer's TurboPass invitations.
Only Managers can create and delete users for their organization
Adding Logos to invitations
To add your company logo to all your account TurboPass invitations, please email support@turbopassusa.com with a PNG file of your logo.
File should be under 100 MB
Remove any backgrounds before submitting
Image must be at least 250px by 250px
Image cannot be blurry or pixelated

TurboPass Created: No Notification Sent | A TurboPass invitation was created but the customer was not sent a TurboPass invitation.
Customer Notified | The customer has successfully been sent a TurboPass link.  They have not yet interacted with the invitation. Confirm with your customer they have been sent the TurboPass link.
Customer Reminded | The customer has received another invitation as a reminder to complete the linking process.
Customer Authorization in Progress | The customer clicked the link on their invitation.
Customer Agreed to Terms | The customer has accepted the TurboPass terms of service and is in the process of linking their account. This step should take less than one minute.
Transitioned to {screen_name} Screen | Indicates that the customer is or was last on a loading screen. The particular loading screen is indicated in the status. If this status has not changed after several minutes, confirm with the customer they are fully completing the link.
Entering Phone Number | The customer was asked to verify their phone number.
Skipped Phone Verification | The user has opted to not provide their phone number for the data aggregators verification. This only happens if the customer has linked their account with our data aggregator before.
Phone Number Verified | The customer submitted their number for verification.
Invalid Phone Number | The customer entered an invalid phone number to verify. Make sure the customer is entering a valid phone number to continue.
Invalid Password Provided | The customer entered an invalid password. Make sure the customer is entering the correct password to continue.
Searching Institutions | The customer is searching for their financial institution, with the following search query: {Search Query}
Institution Selected (Institution Name) | The customer selected an institution.
Matched Institution | The user selected an institution that was presented as a matched institution.
Employer Selected | The customer selected an employer.
Institution Currently Down | The connection to the selected institution is down. Please consider other report types or contact TurboPass support for more details.
Institution Not Supported | We are unable to connect to your customer's selected financial institution at this time. Please consider alternative report types or methods of verification.
Selecting Brand | The customer selected a brand from an institution with multiple online portals.
New Institution Connection | The customer is connecting to a different institution.
Session Expired | The customer did not complete a step in the Link flow and it timed out. Have the customer re-enter their information to link their accounts.
Login Required | The financial institution indicated that the customers password or MFA information has changed. They will need to reauthenticate it by logging into their account on a desktop and on their mobile device
Institution Not Responding *Version 1* | The customer's selected institution is currently not responding to TurboPass servers. Please consider rebuilding the report or an alternative method of verification.
Institution Not Responding *Version 2* | The customer's selected institution is currently not responding to TurboPass servers. Please consider alternative methods of verification.
Institution Not Supported *Version 1* | The selected institution does not currently provide online bank access.
Institution Not Supported  *Version 2* | The selected institution is no longer supported. Please consider alternative report types or methods of verification.
Password Reset Required | The selected institution is requiring the customer to reset their password. Have the customer proceed with the reset process and then return to the TurboPass link to finish completion.
Exited Before Completion | The customer has exited the link without fully completing the institution login process (includes ID, Password and/or Multi-Factor Authentication required by the institution). Please contact your customer to ensure they are completing the process to link their account(s).
Institution Login | The customer is being directed to the institution's website to complete the login process.
Unable to Reset Password | The payroll provider is preventing the customer from resetting their password because our records indicate that the customer has not created their account yet. Have the customer create their payroll account through the payroll provider\'s website and try again.
Invalid Credentials | The customer did not enter the correct credentials during the login process. Try having the customer log in to their institution's mobile app and confirm the credentials are correct, or reset their password and try again.
Insufficient Credentials | The customer has entered an incorrect username and/or password. Please have the customer go to their institution's mobile app or website and confirm their credentials are correct and try again.
Invalid Updated Username | The username provided by the customer did not match the original username provided at the initial connection. Have the customer entering the bank account username again. If they recently changed it, you may need to un-link your account and then re-link.
Communication Error | The selected institution is not communicating with TurboPass servers at this time. Attempt to rebuild the TurboPass report or have the customer connect their account on a desktop for a better connection.
Communication Error | same as above
Institution Login Incomplete | The customer has exited their institution's authentication without completing the login process.  Please contact your customer to ensure they have completed this process.
Insufficient Credentials | The customer has entered an incorrect username and/or password. Please have the customer go to their institution's mobile app or website and confirm their credentials. They can also choose the 'Forgot my password' option within the TurboPass invitation
Failed Authentication with (Institution name) | The customer encountered an error while completing the institution’s authentication process. Please have your customer access the link from a desktop computer to complete the login process.
Incorrect Recaptcha | The reCaptcha authentication failed. Please have the customer try again.
MFA Not Supported | Multi-factor Authentication is not currently supported with this institution, but is required as part of the connection request.
MFA Requested | The institution requested the customer authenticate using multi-factor authentication. This is typically a security question, or a one-time password sent to the email/phone number associated to the account for verification.
MFA Invalid Information | The customer has entered incorrect credentials during multi-factor authentication.  If the issue persists, have the customer log into their institution's mobile or desktop app and change their MFA types. This is commonly found in the Security/Privacy settings.
MFA Submitted | The customer has completed the institution\'s multi-factor authentication process.
Item Submitted | The consumer has submitted an item as part of the verification process.
Successful Connection (with institution) | The customer successfully authenticated with the institution. Please wait a few moments for us to start building your report.
Intake Complete | The customer has successfully completed their intake process. Please wait a few moments for us to start building your report.
Account Locked | The customer has attempted to log in to their account without success too many times. The institution has temporarily locked their account. Have the customer call or log into their institution\'s mobile app or website to unlock the account.
Information Not Found | No customer information was found with the selected institution.
No Transactions Found | No transactions were found for the selected institution. Make sure the customer has tried to connect to the Bravo report.
No Accounts Found | No Accounts Found
Customer's Data Unavailable | No data was found or the requested data is not currently available. Check with the customer to see if they connected the right accounts. If the issue persists, reach out to our support team.
Access Not Granted | The consumer did not grant access to their information
User Setup Required | The institution is requiring the customer to finish setting up their account. Have the customer log into the institution\'s mobile app or website to complete the required set up.
Request Expired | This request has expired. All Identity verification requests expire after 24 hours. To continue, send the customer a new invitation.
Payroll Connection Begun | The connection with the Employer was successful.
Customer Consent | The customer was asked to consent to the privacy policy.
Disclose Data | The data types being shared were disclosed to the customer.
Customer Consent | The customer has consented to sharing the requested data types with TurboPass.
Report Generation Failed | This is an uncommon status, please reach out to TurboPass support at 1-855-667-8477.
Processing Results | The consumer has successfully completed their verification. TurboPass AI is currently processing the results.
Building Your Report | The Customer has successfully linked their account. TurboPass is building your report. This should take 1-5 minutes.
Rebuilding Report | The report is being rebuilt. Please give 1-5 minutes for this to complete.
Automatic Rebuild Attempt | We are attempting an additional rebuild of your report, as certain errors can be resolved automatically by our system.
Automatic Rebuild Success | The automatic rebuild succeeded.
Automatic Rebuild Failure | The automatic rebuild failed.
SnapCheck Data Ready | SnapCheck Data Ready
Report Ready | TurboPass has successfully built the report, and the report is now ready for viewing.
Uploaded to RouteOne | The TurboPass report has been uploaded to the respective RouteOne Deal Jacket.
Connection Invalidated | The customer has had the link opened longer than 30 minutes. Have the customer click the TurboPass invitation again and start over.
Login Limit Detected | An excessive amount of login attempts was detected. This may occur if a user enters their credentials incorrectly too many times in a row. Have the customer confirm their credentials and resend the invitation.
Generic Error | An undetermined error has occurred. Send a new invitation and have the customer try again. If this error continues, contact TurboPass support.
Report Archived | This TurboPass has been archived. Please contact us to un-archive this report.

chunks.source: Informativ's SmartPencil - Your Desk's New Cheat Sheet
chunks.filename: Informativ's SmartPencil - Your Desk's New Cheat Sheet.pdf
chunks.content:
YOUR DESK’S NEW CHEAT SHEET:
BUILT FROM DEALER FEEDBACK
ON FASTER, CLEANER PENCILS
The Desk is under more pressure than ever as today’s dealerships sit at the intersection of
competing forces: consumers demand transparency, lenders demand accuracy, regulators
demand compliance, and dealers demand profit.
And yet, most desks are still relying on tribal knowledge, manual calculations, and legacy tools
that weren’t built for today’s complexity. The result? Slower deals, rewrites, lost gross, and
inconsistent customer experiences.
In this white paper, we share the top takeaways from dealers about their desk challenges and
share how Informativ’s SmartPencil solves them.
THE HIDDEN PROBLEM: THE DESK PERFORMANCE GAP
During on-site dealer visits, one pattern showed up again and again: One A-player desk manager…
and everyone else trying to catch up.
What dealers told us:
Veteran desk managers “know it all in their head”
Newer desk managers require constant coaching
Senior managers spend too much time double-checking deals
Results vary wildly depending on who is at the desk
Why this matters:
Inconsistent pencils create rewrites
Rewrites slow funding and erode trust
Variability increases compliance risk
Key takeaway:
The desk needs the built-in consistency of SmartPencil, not more tribal knowledge.
WHY EARLY PAYMENT ACCURACY MATTERS MORE THAN EVER
Consumers today are shopping online first, seeing “estimated” payments everywhere, and comparing
multiple dealerships before stepping into a showroom.
Dealer concerns:
Showing inaccurate payments too early is off-putting
Racing to the lowest payment creates a bidding war
Desk managers need to know the real payment range—without exposing it prematurely
The insight:
Dealers don’t want to show the best payment immediately. They want to know the best payment immediately.
Key takeaway:
Informativ’s SmartPencil provides payment accuracy – which is a desk tool, not just a sales tool.

AUTOMATE THE FIRST PENCIL—WITHOUT SLOWING THE SALE
Dealers consistently pointed to the same breakdown in workflow:
Salesperson leaves the system
Desk manager manually builds the first pencil
Deals stall
Customers wait
Errors creep in
What dealers wanted:
Faster first pencils
Accurate lender scenarios
No added friction for salespeople
No replacement of existing desking tools
Key takeaway:
Informativ’s SmartPencil is instant, accurate, and consistent—not manual.
THE POWER OF “WHAT IF?” DESKING
One of the most valuable discoveries from dealer feedback: desk managers don’t need one answer.
They need all the options because different lenders buy different deals, small changes (term, cash
down, trade value) change approvals, and incentives, markups, and tax rules vary constantly.
What dealers found valuable:
Instantly seeing best loan and lease options
Dynamically adjusting deals in real time
Understanding profit impact before negotiations begin
Key takeaway:
Faster decisions come from seeing every viable scenario.
Informativ’s SmartPencil calculates them all – instantly.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

STANDARDIZATION ISN’T ABOUT LOWEST PAYMENT—IT’S ABOUT FAIRNESS
Insight from dealer conversations: Some stores intentionally do not show the best payment first.
Why?
Fair lending
Disparate impact concerns
Consistency across every customer
What dealers needed:
A standardized “first pencil”
Same terms, same rates, same presentation
Confidence the desk still knows the full profit range behind the scenes
Key takeaway:
Informativ’s SmartPencil provides compliance and control without limiting profitability.
GUARDRAILS MATTER—ESPECIALLY FOR TOUGH CREDIT
Every desk manager can structure a prime deal. The real challenge? 550 FICO, limited cash down,
high LTV scenarios.
Dealer feedback was clear:
Only one or two people can desk these deals correctly
Everyone else guesses—or overcorrects
Mistakes lead to declined apps and rewrites
What helped:
Lender-specific rules built into the process
Alerts when deals exceed lender limits
Clear guidance on required adjustments
Key takeaway:
SmartPencil’s guardrails don’t slow deals—they prevent dead ends.
COMPLIANCE ISN’T A SEPARATE STEP—IT’S THE BYPRODUCT OF PROCESS
Dealers consistently emphasized the need for a clean audit trail, visibility into every pencil shown,
and proof of what was presented and when.
What mattered most:
Every pencil saved automatically
Stored directly in the deal jacket
Clear chain of custody
No extra steps for the team
Key takeaway:
Informativ’s SmartPencil is built into the compliant workflow, so it’s never a burden.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

What Dealers Ultimately Want
After dozens of conversations, the message was consistent.
Dealers don’t want another disconnected tool, another
manual process, another system to train around.
Dealers do want:
Faster deals
Fewer rewrites
Protected gross
Consistent desking
Built-in compliance
Flexibility to operate their way
The desk doesn’t need to work harder. It needs to work smarter with Informativ’s SmartPencil.
When payment accuracy happens early, lender rules are automated, profit visibility is instant, and
compliance is automatic dealers win more deals with less friction.
READY TO ELEVATE YOUR DESK AND UNCOVER PROFIT?
Click here to learn how SmartPencil turns all of your Desk
Managers into “A” players and ensures every deal is profitable.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — Channel Partnership Program Overview
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — Channel Partnership Program Overview

Informativ Payments is Informativ's channel partnership product that extends its trusted credit, compliance, and customer insight infrastructure directly into the payments layer of automotive commerce. The product enables software partners (CRMs, marketplaces, ad tech platforms, digital retailing tools, AI chat providers) to embed real, credit-qualified, VIN-specific monthly payments into their platforms via REST APIs.

WHO IS INFORMATIV (FOR PARTNER CONVERSATIONS):
Informativ powers thousands of dealerships and partner platforms, processing millions of credit transactions each year. The company's core business is built on trust and compliance. Informativ Payments extends that foundation into the payments layer — bridging the gap between credit decisioning and the front-end car-shopping experience. Informativ calculates real, credit-qualified monthly payments in real time at the VIN level. These are not estimates — they are transaction-grade, fundable payments.

THE PROBLEM INFORMATIV PAYMENTS SOLVES:
The average new car is over $50,000. Interest rates are up, loan terms are longer, and affordability is the biggest barrier to closing deals. Shoppers want to understand what they can actually afford before they click 'contact dealer.' Most dealer websites, CRMs, and digital retail tools are built around price, not payments. A customer falls in love with a car at a $600 monthly estimate online and then finds out it's $790 in-store. That expectation-to-reality gap kills trust, kills deals, and kills the digital journey. Informativ Payments replaces "estimated" with "qualified" — showing real, fundable payments calculated from actual lender programs, real incentives, and live dealer pricing.

TWO AUDIENCES — TWO VALUE PROPOSITIONS:
Page 1 (Dealer Benefits): Higher engagement from 1st touch to final pencil, more conversions, increased sales efficiency, maximize profit through smart deal structuring, build consumer trust, credit-informed payments combining credit/compliance/fraud/transactable payments into a single platform.
Page 2 (Partner Benefits): Modern tech stack (REST APIs), easy integration, cost-effective, low latency/high scale, usage-based pricing (VIN-based — pay only for VINs you need), synchronized with lenders and incentives (updated hourly).

Informativ Payments — How It Works (Technical and Business)

HOW IT WORKS:
The Informativ Payments API connects to real-time lender programs, OEM incentives, and dealer pricing. A partner system (CRM, marketplace, digital retail tool) sends a VIN, zip code, and a few other parameters. Within milliseconds, Informativ returns a credit-qualified monthly payment for both loan and lease scenarios, while ensuring dealership profitability is maximized.

Partners can power every touchpoint with a fundable payment — from a marketplace search result, to a CRM follow-up text, to a chat conversation with an AI agent. It's not just an API — it's an engine for trust that sits behind every digital experience and makes it feel more intelligent, more personalized, and more human.

KEY TECHNICAL DIFFERENTIATORS:
- REST APIs, modern cloud-native architecture, super-fast response times
- Most partners are up and running in days, not weeks
- First in automotive to offer usage-based pricing — pay only for VINs you need payments for. No bloated minimums, no setup costs, no expensive flat rooftop-based fees
- Built on Informativ's compliance backbone — payment calculations already aligned with FCRA, lender guidelines, and dealer-level profit controls
- Engine updates incentives, taxes, lender programs, and dealer rules in near real-time and responds in milliseconds. No stale data, no caching delays, no performance drag

PARTNER OUTCOMES:
- Higher session time on page, driving much better engagement
- Better lead-to-sale conversion
- Improved dealer ROI per user

VS. LEGACY COMPETITORS (OfferLogix / MarketScan):
Historically, payments meant OfferLogix or MarketScan — solid for their time, but expensive, slow, and disconnected from real credit workflows. Informativ Payments is modern, faster, more accurate, and far more cost-effective than legacy payment engines. Built on real credit and compliance infrastructure, not retrofitted old desking systems.

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — Use Cases by Platform Type
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — Where It Fits: Use Cases by Platform Type

AD TECH / MARTECH / SEARCH:
Payment-qualified ads that target shoppers based on affordability, not just geography or make/model. Ads that are dynamic, updating programs and incentives in real time. Most auto ads still show MSRP, but shoppers click when they see what they can actually afford. Informativ lets ad units return real, credit-qualified payments with one API call — driving higher CTR, lower CPA, and better-quality traffic.

DEALER WEBSITES / MARKETPLACES:
Sites like CarGurus, Dealer Inspire-style platforms that want to display real payments next to every VIN. Sites can now offer a true shop-by-transactable-payment experience. Shoppers bounce when site payments don't match what they actually qualify for. Informativ returns real, credit-qualified, VIN-specific payments via soft pull, boosting VDP engagement and lead quality with numbers consumers trust.

CHATBOTS / AI ASSISTANTS:
AI chat platforms can answer "what would my payment be?" in real time with a real number. Most chatbots can't answer the #1 question. Even LLMs like OpenAI and Anthropic break when questions around vehicle monthly payments are surfaced in chat instances. Informativ's payment APIs are built to be easily ingested into AI products, delivering credit-qualified payments in-chat via soft pull, boosting engagement and delivering higher-intent, payment-ready leads.

CRM / BDC / SALES TOOLS:
BDC agents and sales teams can lead follow-ups with real payment offers instead of generic outreach. CRM follow-up texts and emails powered by fundable payments.

DESKING / DIGITAL RETAILING:
Deals fall apart when online payments don't match in-store. Informativ powers real, fundable, credit-qualified payments directly inside desking workflows, reducing re-desks, improving F&I handoff, and protecting dealer margins.

LEAD GEN / NURTURE / CDP / EQUITY MINING:
First-response teams, CDPs, and equity mining platforms can embed real payment context into every touchpoint across the customer lifecycle.

FULL FUNNEL COVERAGE:
Marketing → Lead Gen → Nurture → Sales Process → Deal. Informativ Payments covers the entire automotive commerce funnel from first ad impression to final desk.

chunks.content:
Informativ 2026 SWOT Pro Report
(Version: 12-11-2025)
Instructions: Before the upcoming strategic planning session, take 20–30 minutes to thoughtfully review
your team’s SWOT Pro Assessment results summarized in this report. The categories include Strengths,
Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats, along with insights into competitive positioning and digital
readiness. Your task is to identify key themes, opportunities, issues, and questions to bring forward during
the planning meeting. Then, brainstorm potential action items, priorities, or strategic adjustments that will
strengthen the organization’s position and guide the year ahead.
QUESTIONS OR CLARIFICATIONS FROM THE SURVEY TO DISCUSS AT THE PLANNING MEETING:
PRIORITY AND ACTION ITEM BRAINSTORM BASED ON SURVEY RESPONSES:
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 1

SWOT Pro Assessment – AI-Generated Summary
The following section was generated by AI based on the team’s responses to the SWOT Pro
Assessment. Using natural language analysis, the AI identified the most common themes and
summarized the top five Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats shared across
participants.
Please review these summaries and compare them against the full individual responses in the
Appendix section that follows. Use your judgment to validate, refine, or challenge the AI’s
interpretation as part of the strategic discussion.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 2

Strengths and Differentiators
1. What are the top five Strengths of your organization today?
STRENGTHS ("Things to protect")
1) Customer-Centric Relationships and Retention – We maintain exceptional satisfaction and trusted
partnerships that drive renewals, referrals, and meaningful expansion revenue.
2) Compliance-Native, Integrated Platform – Our differentiated end-to-end solution is essential for dealers’
credit, compliance, and fraud needs, delivering high, scalable value.
3) Deep Automotive Expertise and Credibility – Industry veterans, proven workflows, and strong bureau/dealer-
group partnerships provide authority and access that accelerate adoption.
4) Agile Innovation and Reliable Execution – We rapidly build, iterate, and pivot while consistently delivering
quality outcomes on time across products and operations.
5) Strong Leadership and Collaborative Culture – A smart, mission-driven team with an open-door, roll-up-
sleeves ethos enables alignment, resilience, and sustained performance.
2. Describe your organization’s competitive advantage—the key differentiator or promise
that makes you stand out in the marketplace.
Dominant themes and tone:
- Unified, end-to-end platform: A single source of truth connecting identity, credit, compliance, fraud prevention,
customer insights, and now accurate, lender-ready payments—from lead to funded deal.
- Compliance-native advantage: “Forced march” compliance that automates and enforces consistent, audit-ready
processes across rooftops, eliminating risk and friction while speeding fully fundable deals.
- Real payments intelligence: SmartPencil/Payments provides exact, compliant pencils with real programs, TTL,
incentives, and structures—driving trust, faster decisions, and higher gross.
- Operational impact: Simplifies workflows, reduces fraud, accelerates sales velocity, and maximizes profit with
earlier, more accurate information.
- Differentiated service model: Dealer-first flexibility, flat/subscription pricing, white-glove onboarding and support,
and a partnership culture grounded in trust and integrity.
- Market posture: Confident, disruptive, proactive—aimed at modernizing automotive retail and turning compliance
into a competitive edge.
Variation in perspectives:
- Some emphasize pricing and white-glove service; others prioritize fraud elimination and payments accuracy.
- A few spotlight GTM agility and cultural values as differentiators; most center on the unified platform and
compliance leadership.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 3

Insightful takeaway:
Lean into “one platform, one compliant payment, one process” as the category-defining promise—own the space
where accurate payments and enforced compliance converge to produce faster, fully fundable, more profitable
deals.
3. From the perspective of your customers/clients/end-users, what is the greatest value
they receive from your product or service?
Customers most value confidence and peace of mind: Informativ enforces compliant, standardized processes that
reduce risk while keeping deals moving fast. The platform is seen as reliable, dependable, and “must-have” for
safeguarding data, preventing fraud, and staying ahead of regulatory change—without adding friction. Speed and
operational consistency are core benefits, with dealers citing faster, safer, more profitable decisions and a uniform
sales flow. Owners/principals emphasize protection and assurance that teams follow required processes; frontline
staff value consistency, ease, and fewer errors. Many also highlight dependable credit tools and the company’s
proactive innovation that anticipates market shifts. Flexibility and customization reinforce adoption across different
dealer sizes and setups. Overall tone: protective, trustworthy, and enabling.
Takeaway: Position Informativ as the dealer’s confidence engine—technology-enforced compliance and fraud
protection that standardizes execution and accelerates every deal, backed by proactive innovation that keeps
clients ahead of change.
4. Do you believe these advantages are growing in value, shrinking, or staying about the
same in the next 12 months? Why?
Most respondents believe the company’s competitive advantages are growing in value over the next 12 months.
They cite accelerating dealership pressures—compressed margins, staffing turnover, rising fraud, and tightening
CFPB/FTC scrutiny—that make an integrated, compliance-native, fraud-mitigating, speed-to-desk solution more
essential.
The most common themes:
- Integrated workflow leadership (identity + credit + compliance + deal structure + lender intelligence) is a clear
point of differentiation as dealers consolidate tech stacks and cut vendors.
- Rising fraud and regulatory complexity increase demand for a unified platform that reduces risk, speeds funding,
and simplifies adoption across rooftops.
- Product expansion (e.g., Payments) and deeper CRM/DMS integrations compound stickiness and value by
embedding more of the dealer workflow.
Differences in outlook:
- Confidence is high that value is “significantly” increasing; however, some note headwinds—credit bureau price
increases, economic/pricing sensitivity, and competitors attempting to copy features—which may require continued
innovation and clear ROI.
Insight: The market is moving toward speed, certainty, and simplified stacks; Informativ’s integrated platform aligns
tightly with that shift, indicating strong positioning—provided the team stays ahead on integration depth,
fraud/compliance capabilities, and demonstrable cost/ROI amid rising bureau costs.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 4

Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 5

Weaknesses and Digital Adoption Gaps
5. What are the top five Weaknesses of your organization today?
WEAKNESSES ("Things to eliminate or fix")
1) Product and Engineering capacity constraints slow delivery and limit market responsiveness –
Insufficient resourcing and cohesion in product/engineering reduce velocity and throughput, causing delays
against market demand and slowing innovation.
2) Speed over process creates rework, quality issues, and burnout – Operating too fast with too many parallel
initiatives prioritizes urgency over planning, leading to fire drills, rushed execution, avoidable errors, and
employee strain.
3) Cross-functional silos undermine alignment and execution – Fragmented decision-making and inconsistent
communication between departments (e.g., Product, Sales, CS, Ops, Engineering) cause confusion,
preventable mistakes, and friction in delivery.
4) Data integrity and systems fragmentation hinder insight and governance – Disparate systems, exceptions
to process, weak auditing/reporting, and difficulty accessing reliable data reduce decision quality, transparency,
and scalability.
5) Role clarity and organizational effectiveness gaps weaken accountability – Misaligned job descriptions,
leadership/SME gaps, and unclear operating model (sales- vs product-led) dilute prioritization, consistency,
onboarding, and accountability.
6. What are the top three systems or processes that could be automated or enhanced with
AI and/or technology?
1. Software Delivery & Quality Automation – Use AI to generate test cases, detect bugs from logs, validate
configurations, orchestrate releases, and auto-update documentation to accelerate launches and improve reliability.
2. Customer Support, Onboarding & Success Automation – Deploy AI chat/self-service, intelligent routing, and
predictive health/churn alerts to resolve routine requests, guide adoption, and proactively retain accounts.
3. Revenue Operations, CPQ & Billing Automation – Apply AI to validate pricing and margins, streamline
quoting/contracting, reconcile invoices, and enforce implementation accuracy across Salesforce/NetSuite for faster,
error-free revenue cycles.
Overall, the team emphasizes end-to-end automation, data-driven accuracy, and self-service to reduce manual
work, speed delivery, and improve customer outcomes.
7. Which systems or processes drain energy or joy from the team? Why?
Common drains on energy and joy
- Unpredictable, opaque release management: last-minute changes, rushed testing, instability, and “fire drills” that
force reactive work and rework.
- Constant priority shifts and parallel initiatives: frequent context switching, moving deadlines, and quick
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 6

turnarounds that create whiplash, reduce recognition, and erode trust.
- Cross-team misalignment: unclear ownership and timelines, Sales overpromising or misreading roadmaps,
Product acting as translator, and repetitive status meetings.
- Fragmented, complex tooling and workflows: too many systems, duplicate data entry, complex/slow CPQ and
quoting, and complicated order/implementation steps.
- Manual reporting and unreliable data: repetitive Excel wrangling, conflicting metrics, and slow decisions due to
low data trust; Finance needs differ from what’s provided.
Patterns
- Workload: reactive mode, context switching, and after-hours strain from instability and shifting goals.
- Communication: missing accountability, unclear handoffs, and repeated meetings instead of clear, shared plans.
- Technology: system sprawl, brittle processes, and poor data hygiene driving manual effort and errors.
Insight
Establish a predictable, transparent operating cadence—single source of truth for priorities, releases, ownership,
and metrics—to reduce reactivity, simplify workflows, and restore focus and morale.
8. On a scale of 0–10, how savvy is your team at using technology effectively?
7.1
9. Where would training or improvement with the current internal tech stack have the most
impact?
- Most common training needs:
- Data and reporting: Snowflake (queries, validation, standards), Power BI dashboards, data literacy across teams.
- Salesforce/CPQ/pricing: role-aligned workflows, shared visibility, reducing rework for non-sales users.
- Basic tool fluency: Excel/Sheets; practical, hands-on product training with sandboxes to boost confidence and
speed.
- Process/tools alignment: Aha!/ADO/Jira usage, product intake/release management, cross-team communication
workflows.
- AI enablement: practical adoption across product lifecycle; developer-focused upskilling.
- Internal platform cohesion: tie Salesforce and internal admin portals; reduce duplicate entry; certifications.
- Patterns:
- Underutilized platforms and reporting bottlenecks due to limited expertise and lack of shared standards.
- Friction from tools designed for one function (Salesforce for Sales) misfitting other teams’ workflows.
- Strong preference for practical, low-friction learning (sandboxes, lunch-and-learns) and raising baseline fluency
for all, including leadership.
- Push toward modernization to improve agility and reduce release risk.
- Prioritization insight:
Focus first on a cross-functional, role-based training program centered on Snowflake + Salesforce/CPQ reporting
workflows with shared standards and hands-on practice; this will unlock the largest gains in speed, accuracy, and
confidence while reducing cross-team bottlenecks.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 7

10. What capabilities or advantages do we need to build to win against our competitors in
the future?
Common themes across like-minded responses
- Unified, AI-native data foundation: Clean, governed Snowflake-based lakehouse; consistent definitions; real-time
dashboards; predictive analytics and pricing intelligence; data moat for approval odds, funding risk, lender
performance.
- AI decisioning and automation: True decisioning/desking engine beyond credit; document/compliance automation;
AI-driven internal operations for speed, quality, and scale.
- End-to-end, integrated Deal OS: Seamless workflow from identity to funding; deep ecosystem integrations
(CRM/DMS/DR/OEM/lenders); single “payment truth”; enterprise controls for groups (permissions, pricing,
compliance, reporting).
- Engineering velocity and agility: Modular architecture; faster release cycles; automated QA; AI-assisted
build/test/docs; stability, scalability, and always-on reliability.
- Margin protection and cost control: Smarter credit/data inputs, bureau blending, predictive COGS, cheaper-path
logic; transparent, stable pricing for groups.
- Configurability and packaging: Customer-defined workflows and modular product bundles; expand/innovate to
avoid commoditization and support diversified revenue.
Short-term priorities (0–12 months)
- Increase product/engineering velocity, platform stability, and automated QA.
- Expand and harden integrations to deliver a seamless end-to-end workflow.
- Stand up governed, unified data with core dashboards and predictive KPIs.
- Automate compliance/doc intelligence; strengthen credit controls and packaging options.
- Begin COGS optimization (data blending, pricing transparency).
Long-term priorities (12–36 months)
- Evolve into an AI-powered, compliance-native Deal OS with true decisioning/desking.
- Build a defensible data moat and enterprise-grade group controls.
- Deepen partner/lender ecosystem to keep all work in one flow.
- Mature margin protection via predictive COGS and inputs control; diversify offerings and acquisitions.
Concise leadership insight
Invest first in a unified, AI-ready data foundation and engineering velocity to deliver an end-to-end, compliance-
native Deal OS—then lock in defensibility with deep integrations and COGS control to win on speed, certainty, and
scale.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 8

Opportunities, Growth, and Disruption
11. What are the top five Opportunities for your organization today?
OPPORTUNITIES ("Things we could pursue")
1) AI-Driven Automation, Decisioning, and Fraud Detection across the Deal Lifecycle – Apply AI/ML to lender
prediction, deal automation, doc review, affordability insights, and productivity. This unlocks faster decisions,
lower risk, and scalable innovation that differentiates the platform.
2) Unified, End-to-End Platform with Deep Integrations and Vendor Consolidation – Expand DMS/CRM/LMS
and real-time API integrations to deliver a single, seamless workflow. This meets dealer demand to reduce tool
sprawl, increases stickiness, and grows share-of-wallet.
3) Payments and SmartPencil as a Core Growth Engine – Accelerate Payments API, dealer-direct payments,
verified stips, and AI-assisted desking within SmartPencil. This creates immediate monetization, defensibility,
and daily reliance that boosts retention and expansion.
4) Trusted Compliance and Identity Security Leadership – Embed compliance, identity verification, and risk
controls in every flow amid rising regulation, fraud, and breaches. This positions the company as the safe,
dependable partner dealers prefer and regulators respect.
5) Strategic Partnerships, OEM Channels, and Targeted M&A – Broaden reach via OEM programs,
data/identity partners, lender networks, and selective acquisitions. This accelerates distribution, enriches the
ecosystem, and strengthens competitive moats.
12. Who is our ideal customer today and what problem do we solve for them?
- Most common ideal customer profile:
- Franchise auto dealerships, especially multi-rooftop groups; also high-volume large independents
- Active desking/F&I, multiple lender relationships, high credit app volume
- Leadership and compliance stakeholders (dealer principals, COOs, compliance officers)
- Need for standardized processes across stores, fewer vendors, and a unified workflow
- Experiencing rising fraud/compliance pressure, rehash, inconsistent SOPs, and high credit spend; want faster
sales without sacrificing compliance or margin
- Primary problems/needs addressed:
- Early identity certainty and fraud resistance
- Fast, accurate, lender-aligned first pencils to reduce rehash and protect front/back-end gross
- Compliance-by-default workflows (audit-safe, FTC Safeguards), consistent SOPs, traceability
- Consolidation of fragmented tools, lower training burden, reduced credit/PII risk
- Increased sales velocity, cleaner funding, better customer trust, and lower credit bureau spend
- Variations/gaps in definitions:
- Scope of ICP: franchise-only vs. including large independents; single rooftop vs. prioritizing multi-rooftop groups
- Offering scope: full compliance + payments platform vs. upper-funnel API
- Payment precision: “lender-accurate/penny-perfect” first pencils vs. tolerance for non–penny-perfect estimates
- Primary value emphasis: compliance/fraud and risk reduction vs. sales speed/gross vs. cost control (flat-rate
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 9

credit spend)
- Positioning: strategic partner vs. point solution/tool
- Insight:
- Commit to a primary ICP of multi-rooftop franchise (and high-volume independent) dealers with active desking
and multiple lenders, and anchor the value proposition on instant, lender-accurate, compliance-native, fraud-
resistant first pencils in a unified workflow—explicitly decide whether penny-perfect payments are core to remove
messaging ambiguity.
13. Which customer segments or markets hold the most untapped potential in the next 12–
18 months?
- Most frequently mentioned near-term growth areas:
- Large Independent Dealers and multi-rooftop/regional groups; mid-size franchise dealers with high desk volume
and outdated tools
- OEM channels: OEM DR programs, endorsed tech ecosystems, and OEM-influenced/mandated dealer networks
- Lenders/Credit Unions/Indirect networks (CUDL-heavy, credit-union-forward)
- Digital/hybrid retailers and marketplaces needing secure, streamlined workflows
- Compliance/fraud-driven segments (high-volume, high-risk regions) and upsell within current base (Verified Stips,
Payments, compliance bundles)
- Emerging markets/segments with expansion potential:
- Non-auto/adjacent verticals (RV, powersports, marine) and select independents
- Subprime/BHPH ecosystems
- Direct-to-consumer OEMs (early but strategic)
- Differences in opinion:
- OEM-first mandates vs. dealer-by-dealer expansion
- Double-down in core auto vs. pushing into non-auto adjacencies now
- Lender/CU channel-led growth vs. marketplace/platform-led demand gen
- Product-led upsell (e.g., SmartPencil) vs. net-new segment acquisition
- One concise focus insight:
- Concentrate resources on large independents and mid-to-large dealer groups via OEM and lender/CU channel
partnerships, while running controlled pilots in non-auto adjacencies.
14. What are the top three sources you rely on for insight about your industry (news sites,
thought leaders, associations, etc.)?
1. Automotive News – Comprehensive, timely coverage of dealership, OEM, finance, and policy developments
valued for authoritative reporting and industry-wide analysis; https://www.autonews.com
2. Car Dealership Guy – Practitioner-led insights on retail operations, used-car and finance trends, and tactics
shared via newsletter and social platforms, valued for actionable, frontline perspective;
https://www.dealershipguy.com
3. NADA (National Automobile Dealers Association) – Trusted association guidance with market data, regulatory
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 10

updates, benchmarks, and education resources, valued as the dealer voice and compliance compass;
https://www.nada.org
Overall, respondents favor authoritative trade journalism, practitioner-driven commentary, and association data for
timely, practical, dealership-focused trend monitoring.
15. What emerging technologies could most impact your industry, and how could your
organization use them to enhance or redefine the value you deliver to customers?
Most-mentioned emerging technologies and how they would enhance or redefine value
- Advanced AI decisioning and predictive models (incl. GenAI): Move from static desking to AI-orchestrated,
fundable deal recommendations; automate risk scoring, lender/program selection, and pricing; reduce bureau pulls
while improving approval accuracy; streamline compliance, sales guidance, training, and analytics to cut time and
errors.
- Digital identity and fraud solutions (biometrics, device intelligence, identity wallets, consortium signals):
Authenticate customers and documents in real time; block fraud earlier; reduce stipulations and reworks; speed
funding and build lender/dealer confidence with auditable trails.
- Real-time data and open finance rails (APIs, open banking, RTP): Ingest alternative data and payment signals to
improve decisions; enable instant approvals and faster cash; deliver transparent, up-to-the-minute status across
lenders.
- Embedded lending and dealer-to-lender infrastructure: Single workflow to submit, optimize, and track funding
across a broad lender network; higher approvals, fewer clicks, and better gross through AI-guided lender matching.
- API ecosystems and end-to-end platforms: Deep integrations with CRM/DMS/fintech to unify credit, compliance,
fraud, and payments; potential M&A/partnerships to complete a true end-to-end offering.
- Immutable records for compliance: Tamper-proof digital deal jackets and audit trails that increase trust and reduce
risk.
Secondary but aligned: CDPs for targeted outreach and Fixed Ops AI for service profitability.
One concise leadership insight
Prioritize an AI-first, identity-trusted, API-native embedded finance platform—invest first in AI
decisioning/orchestration and a robust digital identity/fraud layer, then scale via lender/open-banking integrations to
deliver instant, compliant, fundable deals in one unified workflow.
16. Are there competitors, technologies, or adjacent players that could be acquired or
partnered with to enhance your product/service?
Most frequently mentioned opportunities
- Fraud/Identity Verification: Intellicheck, SentiLink, Point Predictive; broader digital ID/eKYC and AI fraud/document
verification platforms
- Lending/Underwriting/Decisioning: Upstart, AutoFi, Zest AI, Open Lending, Car Capital, Streamline.auto; lender
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 11

connectivity and real-time decisioning
- Data & Automotive Intelligence: JD Power (deeper partnership), Urban Science, Nova Credit
- Titling/DMV/TTL Accuracy: Vitu; auto titling/registration solutions
- Forms/eContracting/Digital Retail/Document Intelligence: eForms, eContracting, digital retail providers
- Core Workflow Integrations: CRM/DMS, LMS (Dealertrack, RouteOne), payments and open
banking/income/employment verification
- Scale Plays: acquisition of a large credit reseller to gain leverage with bureaus
Patterns around strategic fit
- Convergence on an end-to-end, funding-ready workflow from lead → identity → credit → compliance → payments →
lender → funding

Threats and Competitive Risks
17. What are the top five external Threats to your organization today?
THREATS ("Things we should mitigate")
1) Surging credit bureau/FICO pricing and cost inflation squeezing margins and driving client price
sensitivity – This threatens profitability, forces difficult pricing decisions, and increases churn risk as customers
seek cheaper alternatives.
2) Macroeconomic and automotive market volatility reducing dealer budgets and credit demand – Slower
sales cycles and budget cuts can delay purchases, shrink pipeline, and pressure renewals.
3) Intensifying competition and commoditization from incumbents, AI-first entrants, and bundled “free”
tools eroding differentiation – Price undercutting and feature parity risk loss of market share unless innovation
and clear value are maintained.
4) Rapidly evolving regulatory, OEM mandate, and compliance requirements disrupting workflows – Failure
to adapt quickly could increase costs, stall implementations, and expose the company and dealers to penalties.
5) Escalating cybersecurity, data privacy, and third-party ecosystem risks jeopardizing trust and
operations – A breach or critical vendor outage could cause downtime, legal liabilities, and significant
reputational damage.
18. Who are your top three direct competitors?
1. 700Credit – https://www.700credit.com – Automotive-focused provider of credit reports, soft-pull prequalification,
identity verification, fraud prevention, and compliance solutions for dealerships and lenders.
2. National Credit Center (NCC) – https://nationalcreditcenter.com – Offers credit reporting, pre-screening/pre-
qualification, compliance, and data-driven marketing solutions tailored to automotive dealerships.
3. Dealertrack (Cox Automotive) – https://www.dealertrack.com – End-to-end F&I, credit application, compliance,
and digital retailing platform (including the uniFI suite) used by automotive retailers and lenders.
Overall, the competitive landscape centers on automotive credit, compliance, and digital retailing platforms; these
competitors closely mirror your organization’s focus on credit workflows, prequalification, and integrations for
dealerships.
19. What do your competitors do better than your organization—especially things that
customers or clients truly value?
Most common, like-minded themes where competitors outperform and customers value:
- Pricing and bundles: Lower sticker prices, willingness to sell on thinner margins, and bundled credit/compliance
packages that reduce vendor count and perceived TCO.
- Ease and adoption: Familiar, simple workflows with fewer clicks, minimal training, predictable/consistent pricing,
and “good enough” capabilities that feel safe and low-risk.

- Distribution and incumbency: Strong brand recognition, longevity, larger field presence, and enterprise
relationships that create status-quo trust and comfort.
- Integration coverage: Pre-built, plug-and-play integrations across DMS/CRM/DR/F&I, direct lender and OEM
integrations, and access to a higher number of lenders; convenience of living inside existing F&I systems.
- Unified ecosystems: Fully controlled platforms that centralize workflows and reduce friction (e.g., Tekion),
perceived as one-stop shops.
- Specific feature edges and innovation pace: Faster perceived release cycles and stronger point capabilities in
fraud/ID verification and compliance controls.
Customer perception patterns:
- Safety, predictability, and low effort beat advanced breadth; incumbents win on comfort and frictionless fit into
existing stacks.
- “One bill, one system, one login” and transparent, low pricing signal lower risk and faster ROI.
- Integrations that eliminate switching pain are valued more than feature depth.
Concise insight:
Win by removing adoption friction and price uncertainty—offer simpler, transparent bundles with plug-and-play
integrations and predictable workflows—then differentiate with a few standout capabilities (fraud/ID, lender
connectivity) that matter most at the point of sale.
20. What are the alternative solutions customers could choose instead of your

- Most common alternatives:
- Incumbent credit/compliance resellers and lending rails: 700Credit, NCC, Dealertrack (DT), RouteOne, eLend,
eUnify
- DMS/commerce platforms with embedded tools: CDK (incl. Roadster), Reynolds & Reynolds, Tekion (when using
full ecosystem)
- Digital retailing and checkout platforms with embedded credit/compliance: AutoFi, CarNow, Gubagoo, Roadster
- Desking/F&I and payment calc tools: Promax, CDK, Darwin, Offerlogix, Market Scan

- Why customers choose them:
- Convenience and workflow integration via existing platforms or “all-in-one” bundles
- Lower cost for credit pulls/aggregators even if compliance/auditability is weaker
- Familiarity/status quo; perceived simplicity and minimal change management
- Flexibility to assemble point solutions to match specific needs
- Credit is a must-have (easy to swap vendors); compliance is a “nice-to-have” (can be minimized, delayed, or
handled manually)
- Trade-offs observed:
- Bundles = convenience but shallow compliance depth
- Cheapest credit = higher risk, weaker audit trails
- Stitched/manual = integration gaps, operational risk, scalability issues
Takeaway: The competitive risk is less about a single superior rival and more about “good-enough” bundled tools
and low-cost credit paired with dealer inertia; winning requires tighter native integrations, clear TCO/risk reduction

proof, and a compelling reason to move beyond status quo.
21. What external risks or threats (market, economic, regulatory, talent) could have the
biggest negative impact? Why?
- Macroeconomic pressure (rates, affordability, delinquencies, softer auto volumes): Lengthens sales cycles,
tightens dealer budgets, increases price sensitivity, and elevates churn risk.
- Rising bureau/compliance costs and policy shifts (soft pulls, consent, data pricing): Compress margins, reduce
credit pulls, force rapid product changes, and create implementation churn.
- Competitive consolidation and aggressive bundling (platform ecosystems, “good enough” AI desking): Heightens
commoditization risk, pressures pricing, and threatens differentiation and retention.
- Regulatory acceleration and legal exposure: Increases cost to comply, elevates operational load, and raises the
bar for “good” compliance in-market.
- Technological disruption (AI identity, automated decisioning, fraud sophistication) and cybersecurity: Requires
faster innovation to maintain accuracy and trust; breaches threaten brand and sales.
- Talent scarcity in product/engineering/data: Slows roadmap velocity and weakens competitive response.
- Third-party dependency risk (bureaus, DMS, APIs): Creates fragility via pricing power, policy changes, and
integration breakage.
- Dealer/OEM consolidation: Shrinks vendor slots and increases procurement hurdles.
Concise insight: De-risk margins and differentiation by tying price to measurable compliance/fundability ROI, pre-
negotiating bureau/partner terms, and front-loading AI/regulatory readiness so you can out-ship “good enough”
bundles while absorbing cost/regulatory shocks.
22. Of the threats you listed, which could be mitigated by technology or AI—and how?
- Most common tech/AI plays to mitigate threats:
- Reduce credit-data cost/dependency: AI bureau routing and pull-reduction, alternative/augmented credit signals,
lender predictability without (or with less) bureau data, predictive bureau usage/cost modeling, potential score
translation (e.g., Vantage→FICO proxy).
- Automate compliance and adapt to regulation: AI-driven deal monitoring, dynamic rules engines, automated
adverse action/audit artifacts, explainable recommendations to withstand scrutiny.
- Defend margin and win deals via AI orchestration: Evolve SmartPencil into an AI “deal architect” that optimizes
structure, fundability, and profit; real-time coaching, guided workflows, and CPQ/margin guardrails.
- Lower operating cost and talent risk: Workflow automation across sales/ops/support; AI-assisted engineering
(code/test/docs), data pipeline validation, and in-platform guidance to boost velocity.
- Strengthen enterprise resilience and scale: AI fraud scoring and payments intelligence; partner/integration
observability with anomaly detection and auto-failover; automated multi-rooftop onboarding, OEM reporting, and
churn prediction.
- Patterns in readiness/optimism:
- Broad optimism that “AI-first” solutions can mitigate multiple threats if pursued deliberately and quickly.
- Clear appetite to accelerate an existing roadmap (SmartPencil → deal orchestration) rather than invent net-new

• Multi-rooftop groups expect stable, transparent pricing

• Smart blending of bureau data to reduce unnecessary costs
• Predictive COGS models built into Snowflake
• Advanced optimization (e.g., cheapest path logic built into Smart Credit)

uniquely provide an integrated, end-to-end solution that delivers greater value to clients
across multiple dimensions.
Joel Carlucci Our competitors appear to offer more cost-effective solutions and have a larger field
presence, which can give them an advantage in reaching and supporting dealers at scale.
Kent Hensley Currently, Informativ seems to have the upper-hand in total product solution, but
competitors are able to beat us by just slashing prices
Kiristen Sandoval Competitors win today in areas where dealers value simplicity, predictability, and low cost.
700Credit feels "easy" because the workflows are familiar and pricing appears consistent.
NCC attracts budget-focused stores with "good enough" compliance bundles at a cheaper
price point. Dealertrack benefits from living inside the F&I workflow, giving dealers the
convenience of one system even if the tools are limited. Across all three, the common
thread is fewer clicks, lower perceived cost, and less friction—qualities that can feel more
comfortable than adopting something newer and more advanced.
Michael Byrd 1. 700Credit
What they do better (customer-valued strengths):
• Brand Awareness & Market Familiarity
700Credit is one of the most widely recognized credit vendors in automotive. Dealers—
especially independents—already know the name, which reduces friction in the buying
process.
• Perceived "Good Enough" Simplicity
Their platform is simple, lightweight, and familiar to dealer staff.
Customers value the fact that:
• setup is basic
• workflows are shallow but easy
• training is minimal
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 57

• Aggressive, Competitive Pricing
700Credit often wins deals by offering lower-cost credit solutions.
For dealers who prioritize price above process or compliance, this is appealing.
• Broad Third-Party Integrations
Because they've been in the market so long, they integrate with almost every:
• CRM
• DMS
• Digital Retailing tool
• Website platform
Dealers value this plug-and-play flexibility.
Why dealers care:
700 feels "safe," inexpensive, universally compatible, and easy to get started with.
NCC (National Credit Center)
What they do better (customer-valued strengths):
• Enterprise-Level Sales & Relationship Depth
NCC has historically been strong in large franchise groups and OEM programs.
They are seen as a "scalable" credit vendor with reach across big organizations and the
infrastructure to support multi-rooftop enterprise customers.
⸻
• Perception of Stability & Longevity
NCC has been in the automotive credit space for decades, and customers view them as
predictable and steady.
The long-standing brand presence gives dealers a sense of continuity and reliability.
⸻
• Bundled Credit & Compliance Products (Including ProMax & Complete Credit)
NCC frequently packages multiple solutions together, creating the perception of a full-
suite offering:
ProMax Desking gives NCC a well-known, legacy desking and lead-management tool that
many dealers—especially older stores—already recognize and feel comfortable with.
Customers value ProMax because it feels familiar, simple, and "good enough" for basic
desking needs.
Complete Credit reinforces NCC's message of offering a turnkey credit + compliance
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 58

package, including basics like Red Flags, OFAC, adverse action, and secure applications.
Dealers often believe NCC bundles:
• simplify invoicing
• reduce vendor count
• lower the effective cost per rooftop
• provide a "one-stop shop" even if the tools are basic
⸻
• Data & Reporting Familiarity
NCC has legacy reporting structures and credit analytics that many dealer groups have
used for years.
Even though the reporting is not modern, the long-term familiarity reduces training friction
and creates comfort for groups that resist change.
⸻
Why dealers care:
NCC feels enterprise-friendly and "big-group ready," with bundled pricing, legacy comfort,
well-known tools like ProMax, and familiar workflows that make the switch appear
easier—even when better technology exists.
3. Eunifi (by Ramsey Theory Group)
What they do better (customer-valued strengths):
• Deep Integration of Compliance, Fraud Prevention & Digital Retail
Workflows
Eunifi specializes in uniting sales enablement, digital deal-jacket management, and
regulatory compliance in one secure cloud platform. ￼
• Advanced Identity Verification & Fraud Detection Capabilities
Their platform offers features like AI-powered ID verification with document
authentication, selfie + liveness checks, video attestation, and automated deal-jacket
audits. ￼
• Real-Time Analytics & Predictive Insights
Eunifi provides real-time sales insights, profit analysis, multi-rooftop visibility, and
aggregated intelligence for dealer groups and OEMs. ￼
• Appealing to Digital-First Retail Models
By streamlining digital workflows, fraud prevention, and compliance in a single
environment, Eunifi is attractive to dealers leaning into online/hybrid models. ￼
Why dealers care:
Eunifi feels like a modern, all-in-one platform that addresses the most pressing issues
dealers face in the digital retail era—fraud risk, compliance complexity, and the need for
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 59

seamless, audited deal workflows.
All 3 competitors sell at a much lower price.
Nicole Grabos Competitors like Dealertrack and 700Credit often come pre-integrated with a wide range
of DMS, CRM, F&I, and digital retailing tools.
700Credit, Dealertrack, and other established players have larger customer footprints and
longstanding name recognition.
Prashant Balgurgi Our competition has better prices to lure new customers
20. What are the alternative solutions customers could choose instead of your
product/service?
Aaron Durkee 700Credit
Tekion (when dealers adopt the full ecosystem)
Digital Retail Platforms w/ Embedded Credit or Compliance Features (Roadster (CDK),
CarNow, Gubagoo, AutoFi)
Identity Verification & Fraud Vendors
Consumer-Facing Prequalification Tools (LeadGen)
Alternative Desking & F&I Tools (Promax, CDK, DT, Darwin)
Brandon Etchison eLend NCC or 700 credit have competing solutions on compliance
offerlogix and marketscan on the payment API
Christina Wofford 700Credit, NCC, eUnify
Darin Larsen They could stitch together several different solution providers to create their own
customized version of what we offer
David Carner not many..cobbled together and poorly integrated systems or doing little to control
compliance and taking the risks
David Frontiera In most cases, many dealers can stick with their current workflow and comfort level.
There are also scenarios where dealers opt for a simpler "transactional and the way we've
always done it approach", because the value they see in product doesn't exceed the value
they see in comfort and status quo.
Emily Milutin he market has multiple credit resellers, and since dealers need to run credit on
consumers, access to a credit service is a core operational requirement. Dealers can
choose from various resellers or work directly with credit bureaus.
In contrast, compliance services are not an absolute necessity for dealers. They can
manage compliance internally by implementing their own policies—or in some cases,
choose not to prioritize it. Therefore, compliance represents a "want" rather than a "need,"
adding value but not serving as a fundamental operational requirement.
Joel Carlucci Dealers may turn to manual processes, competitive vendors, or basic built-in tools—or
choose to delay compliance efforts altogether. Each alternative carries trade-offs in cost,
risk, and long-term value, which positions Informativ as the smarter, more scalable
solution.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 60

Kent Hensley Use another credit reseller, fraud/compliance solution or desking product (ex: NCC has
credit, fraud, compliance and Promax)
Kiristen Sandoval Our top competitors are 700Credit, NCC, Dealertrack, and RouteOne. Dealers also have
the option to piece together alternatives—going directly to the OFAC site or using point
solutions like Intellicheck or IDScan instead of leveraging our full platform.
Michael Byrd 700credit, NCC, Eunifi, Dealer Concierge
Nicole Grabos Dealertrack, Reynolds & Reynolds, CDK, RouteOne, Darwin, AutoFi offer basic or bundled
compliance, ID verification, or credit-pull tools baked directly into workflows.They might
choose that solution because of the convenience of an all-in-one vendor.
Cheaper Credit-Pull Providers or Low-Cost Bureau Aggregators: Dealers may prioritize
reducing bureau spend and opt for the lowest-cost provider, even if compliance and
auditability suffer (700Credit, NCC).
Prashant Balgurgi Customers could manage compliance manually or build inhouse solutions or rather pick a
cheaper solution with known flaws
21. What external risks or threats (market, economic, regulatory, talent) could have the
biggest negative impact? Why?
Aaron Durkee Economic & Credit Market Volatility
Talent Scarcity in Product, Engineering, and Data Science
Third-Party Dependency Risk (Bureaus, DR platforms, DMS vendors, API changes)
Competitive Consolidation by Platform Ecosystem Players
Technological Disruption—AI Identity Verification & Automated Decisioning
Brandon Etchison Bureau cost structure. The increased costs could reduce the number of credit pulls or
cause us to constrain our margin.
Christina Wofford Market and economic
Darin Larsen After a data breach or cybersecurity threats, and competition, I'd say changes in the
automotive industry regarding how credit is used and to what extent
David Carner same as above: credit price increases, lawsuits, breaches, etc.
David Frontiera The external threats with the biggest potential downside are those that squeeze dealer
budgets, compress differentiation, or change the economics of credit/compliance. First,
market and economic volatility (rates staying high, OEM incentive swings, or softer auto
volumes) can lengthen sales cycles and make dealers more price-sensitive, which raises
competitive risk even when value is strong. Second, competitive desk-automation
acceleration is a real threat: if well-funded rivals ship "good enough" AI payment/desking
tools and bundle them cheaply, we face commoditization pressure unless we stay clearly
ahead on fundability, compliance, and profit optimization. Third, regulatory or bureau-
policy shifts around soft pulls, consumer consent, or credit data pricing could raise our
cost base or force rapid product changes, creating margin risk and implementation churn.
Emily Milutin Market position, regulatory changes, price increases, and economic constraints all have
the potential to significantly impact our business. Each of these factors could influence
our success or create substantial challenges. To stay ahead, we must continue enhancing
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 61

our market value, maintain pricing that reflects both value and affordability, and remain
mindful of the regulatory and economic pressures affecting our clients.
Joel Carlucci The biggest external risks include regulatory changes, dealership budget cuts, tech talent
competition, aggressive pricing from competitors, and cybersecurity threats. Each could
impact our growth, customer retention, or brand trust if not proactively managed.
Kent Hensley 1) High interest rates and vehicle prices - impacts consumer affordability
2) Rising loan delinquency - impacts consumer credit worthiness and ability to purchase
new vehicles
3) Complexity of Fraud - must stay on the cutting edge to ensure our solutions are
comprehensive and complete
4) Rising inventory levels - impacts front end margins, but may swing back to buyers
market
Kiristen Sandoval The biggest risks I see are the rising bureau and compliance costs, the continued volatility
in the automotive market, and the regulatory pressure that keeps increasing every year.
These shifts cut into dealer budgets, impact deal flow, and raise expectations around what
"good" compliance looks like
Michael Byrd 1. Rising bureau & FICO costs that directly threaten margins.
2. Rapid regulatory changes requiring fast, resource-heavy adaptations.
3. Dealership consolidation & OEM influence reducing vendor footprints.
4. AI-native competitors & fintech disruptors attacking portions of our
workflow.
5. Technical talent scarcity slowing innovation and product velocity.
Nicole Grabos Rising Credit Bureau Costs
- Dealers become more price-sensitive and may reduce their tech stack.
- Competitors offering bundled or "cheaper" solutions become more appealing.
- Informativ's value must increasingly be tied to cost reduction or risk being seen as
optional.
Prashant Balgurgi Staying ahead of the competition may be challenging with what the company is trying to
achieve in a short period of time. we may be left behind if we do not adapt to emerging
technology
22. Of the threats you listed, which could be mitigated by technology or AI—and how?
Aaron Durkee SmartPencil lender prediction AI that becomes industry-leading
AI-Driven Credit/Lender Predictability Without (or With Less) Bureau Data
AI-driven workload automation lowers operational costs (for Informativ AND dealers)
AI-Driven Dealer Sales Enablement
- Buyer Persona & Intent Predictions
- AI-Powered Appointment and Lead Re-Engagement
- Intelligent Workflow Automation That Reduces Salesperson Admin Work
- Real-Time Deal Coaching for Desk Managers & Salespeople
- Automated Sales Scripts, Objection Handling & Compliance-Friendly Messaging
Brandon Etchison If we could find a way to convert vantage score to a fico with a reasonable algorithm or AI
data set.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 62

More AI first solutions could mitigate or at lease minimize all threats mentioned above.
Christina Wofford
Darin Larsen Not sure how AI can be used to mitigate our known threats, but it certainly could be used
to enhance our product functionality and perceived value
David Carner none
David Frontiera Several of those threats can be meaningfully mitigated with technology/AI if we're
deliberate about it. Competitive desk-automation pressure is best countered by
accelerating our own AI roadmap: evolve SmartPencil into a true AI "deal orchestration"
engine that recommends the most fundable, profit-optimized structures with
explainability—so we stay clearly ahead on outcomes, not just speed. Regulatory/bureau-
policy shifts can be softened by building configurable rules engines and AI-assisted
compliance monitoring that adapt quickly to new consent, pull, or pricing requirements,
plus predictive modeling to forecast cost impacts before they hit margin. Integration
dependency risk can be mitigated with better observability and resilience: AI-driven
anomaly detection for partner outages, automatic failover paths where possible, and
proactive dealer messaging triggered by telemetry so trust isn't lost in the gap. Even
market/economic volatility can be partially offset by AI-validated CPQ and margin
guardrails that predict dealer usage, flag off-margin drift early, and recommend package
adjustments proactively—helping us protect margin and defend value when budgets
tighten.
Emily Milutin Our market position can be strengthened through technology, and we are already making
progress in this area. By leveraging technology to enhance our offerings, we can provide a
more robust client experience and accelerate onboarding. A top-tier product combined
with a short implementation timeline delivers significant value—clients want to start using
what they've purchased immediately. Continued investment in technology to drive both
internal efficiency and external service growth will be critical to our ongoing success.
Joel Carlucci AI and automation can mitigate key threats like regulatory changes, cybersecurity risks,
economic pressure, and talent gaps by improving efficiency, reducing risk, and enabling
smarter, faster decisions across the business.
Kent Hensley 1) Informativ payments technology assists with consumer affordability and more options
for consumers to purchase
2) Informativ's fraud solutions mitigate risks for dealers and lenders
Kiristen Sandoval Rising bureau costs can be offset by better decisioning and fraud tools that help dealers
pull fewer bureaus. Regulatory pressure becomes easier to manage with automated
compliance checks and real-time audit alerts. And the talent gap shrinks when AI supports
teams with guided workflows and training. The more intelligence we build into the
workflow, the less exposed we are to cost, compliance, and capacity risks.
Michael Byrd AI can mitigate 5 major external threats by:
• Optimizing COGS and reducing bureau dependency
• Adapting to regulatory changes quickly and automatically
• Strengthening Informativ's appeal to large dealer groups
• Matching or surpassing AI-native competitors
• Filling talent gaps and increasing development velocity
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 63

1. Rising Credit Bureau & FICO Costs
How AI can mitigate this threat:
• Smart bureau routing & cost optimization
Use AI to automatically determine the lowest-cost bureau pull per customer profile, lender,
or deal type.
• Predictive bureau usage modeling
Forecast bureau consumption and cost spikes to improve budgeting and negotiation
leverage.
• Alternative data & bureau blending
Introduce machine-learning-driven credit indicators (cash-flow, income stability, ID risk
score) to reduce reliance on bureau pulls where possible.
Outcome:
AI helps Informativ control and optimize COGS—reducing exposure to bureau pricing
volatility.
⸻
2. Rapid Regulatory & Compliance Rule Changes
How AI can mitigate this threat:
• Automated compliance monitoring
AI can analyze every deal jacket and detect missing steps, outdated forms, or compliance
violations before they occur.
• Dynamic rule engines
AI-powered rule parsing can update workflows automatically as regulations change.
• AI-generated compliance documentation
Automatically generate adverse action summaries, audit logs, policy updates, and change
records.
Outcome:
Informativ can adapt faster than competitors when regulations shift—turning a core threat
into a differentiator.
⸻
3. Dealer Group Consolidation & OEM Influence
How AI can mitigate this threat:
• Automated onboarding & multi-rooftop standardization
AI can detect process inconsistencies across rooftops and auto-recommend standardized
workflows.
• Predictive churn modeling
AI can flag rooftops at risk of cancellation after an acquisition.
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 64

• OEM-level reporting automation
AI can generate compliance and operational reports required by OEMs—strengthening
Informativ's strategic value.
Outcome:
AI makes Informativ the easiest platform for enterprise groups to scale across dozens of
rooftops, reducing the risk of being replaced after acquisitions.
⸻
4. AI-Native Competitors & Fintech Disruption
How AI can mitigate this threat:
• Build AI-driven fraud scoring, payment intelligence, and compliance
automation
Matching or surpassing new entrants on innovation helps Informativ stay competitive.
• Continuous product improvement from usage data
ML models can detect adoption gaps and automated feature recommendations.
• AI-powered dealer coaching & in-platform guidance
Helps differentiate Informativ with proactive support built directly into the platform.
Outcome:
By building AI natively into the platform, Informativ shifts from reactive to innovation
leader.
⸻
5. Technical Talent Scarcity
How AI can mitigate this threat:
• AI-assisted development (accelerated engineering velocity)
Code generation, automated testing, and documentation can reduce dependency on
scarce engineering talent.
• AI-driven data pipeline validation
Automated cleansing, reconciliation, and anomaly detection reduce the need for data-
engineering headcount.
• AI-enabled support automation
Lowers the burden on support and implementation teams, allowing technical staff to
focus on high-value initiatives.
Outcome:
AI fills gaps created by talent shortages and accelerates product delivery despite limited
resources.
Nicole Grabos Rising Credit Bureau Costs
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 65

AI optimization models that recommend the most efficient bureau-pull workflows
Automated pull-reduction rules, stopping unnecessary or duplicate pulls
Predictive analytics to reduce credit waste by forecasting deal success likelihood
Informativ can position itself as the dealer's cost-savings engine.
Prashant Balgurgi Improved productivity and faster feature development can be achieved by using Microsoft
CoPilot
Informativ SWOT Pro Assessment – CONFIDENTIAL – Page 66
