CONFIDENTIAL
Informativ
Value Selling Playbook
Aligned to Simon-Kucher Monetization Recommendations
Prepared by: Michael Byrd, Chief Revenue Officer
Date: February 2026
This document summarizes Informativ's current Value Selling program, built in response to the Simon-Kucher Monetization Engagement (May 2025).


# Executive Summary

In May 2025, Informativ engaged Simon-Kucher to develop an optimal monetization strategy targeting $5M–$10M in incremental revenue uplift. A core deliverable of that engagement was a Value Selling program designed to enable the sales team to articulate value clearly, defend prices confidently, and win on outcomes rather than features.
This playbook documents what Informativ has built in response to those recommendations. It is organized around Simon-Kucher's three core value selling pillars — Value Articulation, Objection Handling, and Selling Techniques — and maps each pillar to the specific tools, training content, and enablement assets we have developed.
It also reflects a significant strategic shift in our product packaging: we have retired the legacy credit-first tiers (Foundation and Detect) and replaced them with a SaaS-first tier architecture (Protect, Enrich, Elevate, Control) designed to anchor every customer relationship on the compliance platform, drive higher gross margin, and expand average contract value. All value messaging in this playbook reflects the new tier structure.
What This Document Accomplishes
Demonstrates alignment between our internal work and SK's formal recommendations
Provides Simon-Kucher with full visibility into our current enablement stack
Surfaces gaps and open questions where we are seeking SK input and guidance
Serves as a living reference document for our sales leadership team


# 1. Simon-Kucher Recommendations Framework

Simon-Kucher's core recommendation for the Informativ sales force was captured in a single statement: 'Enable the sales team to sell value and defend prices.' This was operationalized into three interconnected pillars and one channel-specific initiative.


## 1.1 The Three Pillars



## 1.2 GTM Strategy for Independent Dealers

Simon-Kucher identified independent dealerships as a distinct segment requiring a differentiated GTM strategy — including dedicated sales resources, tailored materials, and appropriate incentive structures. Independent dealers operate differently from franchised dealers: smaller operations, less compliance sophistication, higher price sensitivity, and less familiarity with platform-based solutions.
Informativ has developed battle card assets for this segment. A full independent dealer GTM strategy remains in development and is an area where we are actively seeking SK input.


# 2. Value Foundation: The Make More / Save More / Stay Safe Framework

The foundation of Informativ's value selling approach is a three-driver value framework that organizes every product capability into a business outcome category. This was developed internally and aligns directly with SK's Value Articulation pillar.


## 2.1 The E3 Sales Philosophy

Informativ's sales philosophy operationalizes the three value drivers through a structured rep mindset model called E3. This framework was developed as part of our Value Selling Training Program and serves as the cultural foundation for how reps approach every conversation.


## 2.2 Quantified Value Proof Points

SK's Value Articulation pillar emphasizes the need for compelling, evidence-based value stories. The following proof points are embedded in our enablement tools and are used in live sales conversations, proposals, and objection handling.


# 3. Tier Architecture & Value Messaging

One of the most significant structural decisions made since the SK engagement is the retirement of the legacy credit-first tiers (Foundation and Detect) and their replacement with a SaaS-first tier architecture. This shift was directly informed by the SK monetization strategy work and is designed to anchor every customer on the compliance platform, drive higher gross margin, and increase average contract value.


## 3.1 Tier Transition: Then vs. Now



## 3.2 Current Tier Architecture: Protect | Enrich | Elevate | Control

The current four-tier structure is designed to create clear value progression from compliance baseline to full-platform optimization. Every tier includes the compliance platform — there is no credit-only entry point. This is the SaaS-first strategic shift.


## 3.3 Tier Value Messaging by Persona

Each tier's value message must be adapted to the persona receiving it. The table below maps tier value language to the decision-makers who respond to it most directly.


# 4. Persona-Based Discovery (Value Articulation + Selling Techniques)

SK's Value Articulation pillar requires reps to understand customer pain points and use cases before presenting solutions. SK's Selling Techniques pillar requires consultative discovery skills. Informativ addresses both with a structured Persona-Based Discovery system.


## 4.1 Discovery Philosophy

We do not lead with product. We lead with pain. Every discovery call is structured around three questions:
What risks is this dealership carrying that they have not quantified?
What inefficiency is slowing their deal flow or inflating their costs?
What is the cost of doing nothing — and do they know it?
The answers to these questions determine tier alignment. We document discovery findings in Salesforce and use them to build tier-specific proposals with ROI anchored to discovered pain.


## 4.2 Persona Discovery Framework

## 4.3 Compliance Risk & Opportunity Audit

A core discovery tool is the Compliance Risk & Opportunity Audit — a structured template used to walk dealers through a dealership-level compliance assessment. This tool accomplishes three things simultaneously:
Surfaces compliance gaps the dealer has not quantified or even identified
Positions Informativ as a trusted compliance expert, not just a vendor
Creates urgency by connecting specific gaps to FTC enforcement risk, lender liability, and fraud exposure
The audit covers FTC Safeguards compliance, FCRA/credit compliance, fraud prevention posture, and deal jacket documentation practices. The completed audit serves as the foundation for the tier recommendation and proposal.


## 4.4 External Pressures as Value Anchors

SK's Value Articulation framework requires reps to connect product value to real-world pressure. We have developed an External Pressures framework that maps market events to dealer pain and Informativ solutions.


# 5. Feature-Advantage-Benefit Framework (Value Articulation)

SK's Value Articulation pillar requires reps to develop compelling customer value stories — not feature lists. Informativ's Feature-Advantage-Benefit (FAB) framework translates every product capability into a business outcome. This is embedded in rep training and proposal language.


# 6. Objection Handling System (SK Pillar 2)

Simon-Kucher's second pillar focuses on equipping the sales team to defend prices with value evidence and address competitive alternatives. We have built a multi-layered objection handling system organized by objection type, tier, and sales stage.


## 6.1 Objection Blocking: Preemptive Value Language

Before objections arise, reps are trained to block them by establishing expertise and anchoring value in the opening conversation. The following language is used to preempt the most common resistance areas:


## 6.2 Objection Handling by Tier



## 6.3 Objection Handling by Sales Stage

Objections shift in nature as the deal progresses. We have structured our objection handling system by sales stage so reps know what resistance to expect — and how to respond — at each point in the process.


# 7. Value-Based Sales Process (SK Pillar 3: Selling Techniques)

Simon-Kucher's third pillar focuses on coaching consultative selling approaches and value-based negotiation. Informativ's sales process is organized into six stages, each with defined rep actions, value-selling behaviors, and supporting enablement tools.


## 7.1 Negotiation Framework by Persona

Price negotiation must be persona-specific. Each buyer has a different primary concern and a different pressure point. The following framework guides reps and Directors of Sales through value-based negotiation by role.


# 8. Quote Confidence Checklist (Quality Gate)

The Quote Confidence Checklist is a mandatory pre-proposal quality gate that ensures every quote is defensible before submission. It aligns directly with SK's Selling Techniques pillar by enforcing disciplined discovery-to-proposal alignment and preventing premature or misaligned pricing.


# 9. GTM Strategy for Independent Dealers (SK Recommendation)

Simon-Kucher specifically identified independent dealerships as requiring a differentiated go-to-market approach. Independent dealers differ from franchised dealers in meaningful ways that affect every element of the sales and value conversation.


## 9.1 What We Have Built for Independents

Battle card assets tailored for small franchise and independent dealer profiles
Pricing overview materials that emphasize flat-rate simplicity and fraud ROI
Case study: Fremont Toyota (value proof accessible to smaller dealers)
External Pressures framework — FTC Safeguards applies equally to independents and is a powerful urgency lever


# 10. Enablement Stack: Assets Built & SK Pillar Alignment

The following table catalogs all value selling assets currently in Informativ's sales enablement library, maps each asset to the SK pillar it supports, and identifies the stage in the sales process where it is used.


# 11. Recommended File Package for Simon-Kucher Review

The following files are recommended for inclusion in the materials shared with Simon-Kucher alongside this playbook. Each is selected because it provides direct evidence of what we have built in response to SK's recommendations and supports a productive review conversation.


## 12.1 Priority Files

## 12.2 Supporting Files


SK Pillar | What We Built | Status
Value Articulation | Value Driver Map, FAB Framework, Tier Value Messaging, E3 Philosophy, Proof Points Library | Implemented — seeking refinement
Objection Handling | Objection Playbook (by tier + stage + persona), Price Defense Language, Negotiation Guide | Implemented — seeking gap review
Selling Techniques | Persona Discovery Guide, Compliance Audit Template, Quote Confidence Checklist, Stage-Based Process Map | Strong foundation — coaching gaps remain
GTM for Independents | Battle Cards developed; full independent-specific GTM in progress | Partial — seeking SK guidance


Pillar | SK Definition | What This Means for Informativ
1. Value Articulation | Train the sales team to clearly communicate value propositions. Help sales understand customer pain points and use cases. Develop compelling customer value stories. | Reps must be able to connect Informativ's capabilities directly to the dealer's financial and operational pain — not just feature lists. Every conversation should quantify risk avoided, cost saved, or revenue gained.
2. Objection Handling | Equip sales with responses to price objections. Help defend pricing strategy with value evidence. Address competitive positioning and alternative solution concerns. | Price pressure is real — from 700Credit, from bureau cost increases, from dealers conditioned on a la carte pricing. Reps need a repeatable system for anchoring price to value, not conceding to cost.
3. Selling Techniques | Coach sales on consultative selling approaches. Train on value-based negotiation tactics. Develop consultative discovery skills. | Informativ sells to multiple personas within the same dealership. Each persona has a different pain threshold, budget authority, and definition of value. Discovery must be persona-driven, not product-driven.


Value Driver | Business Impact | Informativ Capabilities
Make More | Increase close rate and lead quality. Sell more cars by qualifying buyers earlier, reducing friction at the desk, and accelerating deal speed. | Soft pull pre-qualification, SmartPencil deal desking, Customer Insights dashboard, QR code lead capture, eZ Form Fill, website credit applications
Save More | Reduce operating costs. Cut fraud losses, lower credit bureau spend, and eliminate recontracts that cost time and money. | Soft pull strategy (replace hard pulls), Credit Guardrails, fraud prevention suite, Verified STIPs recontract reduction, flat-rate predictable pricing
Stay Safe | Minimize regulatory, legal, and audit risk. Protect the dealership from FTC enforcement, fraud exposure, and lender buyback liability. | Compliance Platform (Red Flags, OFAC, MLA, Adverse Action), digital deal jackets, synthetic ID detection, eVerify DL scan, SIM validation, FTC Safeguards compliance workflows


Step | E3 Principle | Application
1 | Empathize | Understand the dealer's world before introducing any product. Learn their current pain — fraud exposure, recontract frequency, compliance gaps, credit spend levels. Ask before you tell.
2 | Educate | Connect market pressures (FTC enforcement, fraud surge, bureau cost increases) to the dealer's specific situation. Help them understand the risk they are carrying — and the opportunity they are leaving on the table.
3 | Empower | Give the dealer the information, quantified ROI, and confidence they need to make a defensible decision. A dealer who understands the value of what they are buying does not need to be sold — they need to be empowered.

Feature | Advantage | Benefit (Dealer Outcome)
Compliance Platform (Red Flags, OFAC, MLA, Adverse Action) | Automated workflows enforce compliance on every deal without manual tracking | Reduces FTC enforcement risk and audit exposure. No more 'we missed a step' — the platform enforces the process so you do not have to.
Mobile Driver's License Scan + eVerify | Real-time ID verification with synthetic fraud detection at point of contact | Stops fraudulent deals before they reach F&I. Eliminates the cost of funded fraud and lender chargeback liability.
Soft Pull Credit (mirror-image) | Same data as hard pull — no credit impact, no compliance trigger, no consumer friction | Pre-qualify buyers on the lot without commitment. Reduce hard pull volume and bureau spend. Speed up desk qualification.
SmartPencil Deal Desking | Instant payment calculations integrated directly into the compliance workflow at the F&I desk | Faster presentations, higher PVR, fewer missed opportunities at the desk. F&I manager works smarter, not harder.
Verified STIPs (TurboPass) | Automated income and employment verification replaces manual stipulation collection | 45% fewer recontracts. Faster funding. Less back-and-forth with lenders. F&I manager's time recaptured.
Customer Insights Dashboard | Real-time buyer intelligence — credit tier, buying potential, pre-qualification status — surfaced to the sales floor | Reps work qualified leads instead of guessing. Close rate improves. Wasted soft pull pulls are eliminated.
Credit Guardrails | Usage controls and alerts that prevent unauthorized or excessive hard pull activity | Protects the credit bureau budget. Managers maintain oversight of pull activity without micromanaging every rep.
Digital Deal Jacket + eSign | All deal documents captured digitally and stored in a secure, audit-ready format | Compliance is provable. Audits are fast. Paper chaos is eliminated. Funding is accelerated.


Topic | Blocking Language
Compliance | "Most dealers believe they are covered — until a lender or auditor asks for documentation, or a deal gets kicked back. Informativ captures everything digitally so there is never a question of whether you followed the right process."
Price / ROI | "Most of our dealers see Informativ pay for itself within the first few months — whether from catching a single fraud case, converting more leads with soft pulls, or saving on bureau fees. We designed this to deliver measurable ROI, not a cost."
Fraud | "Fraud is getting smarter — more synthetic IDs and mobile-first scams than ever before. Informativ uses multiple data points simultaneously — SIM validation, DL scan, soft pull, OOW questions — to stop fraud before it starts."
Process Change | "Sales teams do not want more tools — they want tools that make their process faster. Informativ was built to work with what they already do: scan a license, pull credit, move through the sale. It is guided, intuitive, and faster than what you are doing now."

# | Objection | Relevant Tier(s) | Response
1 | "We only need credit — why pay for more?" | Protect (entry discussion) | Every dealer needs a defensible compliance process. Protect gives you credit AND fraud AND compliance as a single flat-rate investment. The risk of compliance exposure or one fraud event far exceeds the incremental cost of Protect over a credit-only solution.
2 | "Why is Enrich more expensive than Protect?" | Enrich | SmartPencil is not just a feature — it changes how your F&I desk operates. Faster presentations, better PVR, integrated with your compliance workflow. The investment pays back in the first few deals where a cleaner desk process yields better F&I gross.
3 | "We already use soft pulls somewhere else." | Elevate / Control | Not all soft pulls are equal. Informativ's are mirror-image — same data as a hard pull, no consumer impact, direct API connection for credit app pre-fill. You also get them integrated into the compliance workflow. Fewer keystrokes, more deals desked faster, and one fewer vendor login.
4 | "Control is too expensive for our group." | Control | Control is an operational investment, not a feature upgrade. Multi-rooftop dashboard, complete fraud and compliance stack, hard and soft pull credit, Customer Insights — all in one number. The question is: what does it cost you per month when fraud gets through, a recontract happens, or an audit finds a gap?
5 | "Can we go a la carte instead of a full tier?" | Any tier | The tiers are designed as operational systems, not feature menus. The capabilities within each tier are chosen because they solve problems that appear together. A la carte leaves gaps that typically show up as fraud events, recontracts, or compliance failures — all of which cost more than the tier.
6 | "Our CRM/DMS already handles fraud and compliance." | Protect / Enrich / Control | CRMs and DMSs are workflow tools — they are not built to prevent fraud or enforce compliance. Informativ gives you real-time fraud alerts, DL scan with Red Flag validation, synthetic ID detection, and showroom hard stops that prevent bad deals before they reach F&I. Your CRM shows you the lead. Informativ ensures it is a safe, real one.


Stage | Common Objection | Objection Type | Response Strategy
Prospecting | "We're happy with what we have." / "Not a good time." | Status quo / timing | Introduce an external pressure (FTC, fraud surge, bureau cost increases) that creates urgency without pressure. Plant a question they cannot easily dismiss.
Discovery | "We don't have compliance issues." / "Fraud isn't a problem here." | Awareness gap | Do not argue — ask. 'Walk me through how you handle Red Flags today. What happens when a DL scan flags an issue?' Surface the gap through the question, not the statement.
Demo (TD) | "This looks like a lot of change for our team." | Process resistance | Show the 'Before Informativ / After Informativ' workflow. The friction of change is smaller than the cost of the status quo. Use the ROI calculator in real time.
Pricing | "The price is too high." / "700Credit is cheaper." | Price objection | Anchor to ROI, not features. 'What tier are they proposing, and what does it not include? What is the cost of one fraud event or one audit finding?' Price comparisons require scope comparisons.
Close | "Let me think about it." / "I need to talk to my partner." | Stall / delay | Identify the real objection behind the stall. Confirm value alignment. Use the Quote Confidence Checklist to ensure everything has been addressed. Offer to facilitate the conversation with the partner.

Dimension | Franchised Dealer | Independent Dealer
Compliance Sophistication | Generally higher — OEM compliance requirements, franchise agreements, formal compliance structures | Generally lower — owner-operated, less formal compliance infrastructure, often unaware of FTC requirements
Price Sensitivity | Moderate — budget process more structured, ROI justification expected | Higher — owner makes the call, often views cost as a direct reduction to margin
Fraud Exposure | High — high volume means more exposure, but also more systems to manage it | High and often unmitigated — smaller operations with fewer safeguards, lower awareness of synthetic fraud
Primary Decision Maker | GM, GSM, or CFO (depends on group size) | Owner / Dealer Principal — one conversation, high authority, high personal sensitivity
Tier Entry Point | Protect or Enrich — value on compliance + SmartPencil resonates | Protect — lead with fraud protection and compliance basics; build from there

chunks.content:
Verified Stips Battle Card – Credit-Only Customers
(Not on Informativ Compliance — Using Informativ Credit Only)
Primary Positioning
Verified Stips powered by TurboPass verifies income, residence, and employment instantly, pulling data directly from the source – and it plugs right into RouteOne and DealerTrack — no need for our Compliance platform.
Top 5 ROI Hooks
Prevent Fraud – Verified data direct from source, no chance of falsified docs.
Speed Decisioning & Funding – From days to minutes, improves cash flow.
Modern Customer Experience – No scanning, emailing, or uploads for the customer.
Lender Origination Fee Rebates – Some lenders rebate fees when stips are pre-verified.
No Workflow Change – Fits into RouteOne/DealerTrack just like current stips.
Key SDR Talk Track
“You’re already using Informativ Credit. Verified Stips is a plug-in that lets you send fully verified stips right to RouteOne or DealerTrack — no change to your process, just faster funding, fewer fraud risks, and in some cases, lender rebates.”
Common Objections & Rebuttals
Objection: “We already collect stips directly from customers.”
Rebuttal: “That’s exactly where fraud slips in. Verified Stips pulls data directly from the source, so you don’t have to rely on customer-provided documents.”
Objection: “We don’t want to change our process.”
Rebuttal: “No change needed — your team keeps using RouteOne or DealerTrack the same way, the only difference is the stips arrive pre-verified.”
Closing Question
“If you could prevent fake docs and fund faster without changing your process, is there a reason you wouldn’t look at this?”
Fraud Prevention & Risk Mitigation Hooks
If someone provides a fake pay stub today and it slips past your current process, how much could that cost you in bad paper, repo expenses, and lost time?
You already verify credit profiles—why not add verified income and insurance checks to stop fraud before the deal ever reaches funding?
How confident are you that your current stip review process would catch a high-quality digital forgery before it leaves your lot?
Operational Efficiency & Compliance Hooks
4. If your team could collect and verify stip documents electronically—no personal emails, no photo texts—how much faster and cleaner would your deal jackets be?
5. Fraudsters tend to hit when you’re busiest. How valuable would it be to have a verification step that runs instantly, even on your peak sales days?
6. When a lender asks for proof you verified income or insurance, how valuable would it be to hand over a timestamped, third-party report instead of a stack of paper copies?
Multi-Layered Fraud Approach Positioning
You’re already protecting the credit side of your deals. Adding Verified STIP covers the other major fraud vector—falsified stips—giving you protection from multiple angles. Which point of entry do you think fraudsters will target next?
In today’s market, do you feel safer relying on a single fraud check, or building a layered system that shuts down bad deals at every step?

chunks.content:
Verified Stips Battle Card – Compliance Customers
(On Informativ Compliance or Total Solution)
Primary Positioning
Verified Stips powered by TurboPass is an upgrade to your current secure stip collection process. It automates the verification step — pulling income, residence, and employment data directly from the source in real time.
Top 5 ROI Hooks
Upgrade Existing Security – Already secure, now fully automated verification.
Prevent Fraud – Stops falsified documents before they reach the desk.
Speed Funding – Cuts stip collection from days to minutes, accelerates lender funding.
Lender Rebates – Some lenders waive or rebate origination fees for pre-verified stips.
No Process Change – Uses your existing Informativ Compliance workflow.
Key SDR Talk Track
“You’re already ahead with secure stip collection in Informativ Compliance. Verified Stips is the next step — it eliminates customer uploads entirely by pulling verified data from the source, so deals fund faster and fraud risk drops to near zero.”
Common Objections & Rebuttals
Objection: “We already have a secure system for stips.”
Rebuttal: “Absolutely — Verified Stips builds on that by removing the need for the customer to send any documents at all. It’s about speed and fraud-proofing, not replacing what works.”
Objection: “I don’t want to retrain my team.”
Rebuttal: “No retraining — it’s built into your current workflow, just an added step that runs in the background.”
Closing Question
“Since you’re already secure, why not take the next step and make it instant?”
Fraud Prevention & Risk Mitigation Hooks
If a fraudster slips through your current process today, what would it cost you—in lost inventory, chargebacks, or legal exposure?
We already catch fake IDs and run Red Flags. Wouldn’t it make sense to verify stip documents with the same level of certainty before you fund the deal?
How confident are you that your current stip collection process would detect a digitally altered pay stub or insurance card before delivery?
Operational Efficiency & Compliance Hooks
If your team could collect and verify all stip documents digitally—no texting, no Gmail—how much time and risk would that remove from each deal?
We know the busiest days are when fraudsters strike. What would it mean to have an automated verification step that works just as fast on Saturday at 5 PM as it does on Tuesday morning?
When the FTC or your lender asks how you verified income or insurance, how valuable would it be to pull up a timestamped, third-party validation instead of a photocopy?
Multi-Layered Fraud Approach Positioning
You’ve already got ID verification and compliance checks through Total Solution. Adding Verified STIP means we’re protecting your deals at every point—ID, credit, and now stip verification. Which layer do you think fraudsters will try to break through first?
With fraud on the rise and schemes getting more sophisticated, do you feel safer relying on a single check—or a layered system that shuts the door at every step?

External Threat / Regulation​ | Dealer Pain / Risk​ | Informativ Solution​ | Value Delivered​
FTC Safeguards Rule​ | Risk of $51,744 per violation due to missing ID verification, unsecured PII, or lack of audit trail​ | Informativ’s Compliance Suite, Digital Deal Jackets​ | Automates compliance steps, secures PII, provides audit logs & manager oversight​
FTC CARS Rule​ | Deceptive practices, unclear disclosures → regulatory penalties​ | Digital Credit Apps + Prequal via Customer Insights​ | Enforces transparency & tracks buyer consent/documentation​
Auto Loan Fraud Surge​ | Synthetic identities, fake documents → unrecoverable vehicle losses​ | Fraud Suite: ID Scan + Barcode Check, SIM/Device Verification, Soft Pull Fraud Insights​ | Stops fake identities before the deal, validates buyer authenticity​
CDK Data Breach​ | Operational shutdown, lost consumer data trust​ | Secure Document Collection Portal, PII Encryption & User Controls​ | Protects dealership from ransomware risk & enforces employee data handling security​
State-Level Privacy Laws​ | Exposure to consumer lawsuits over mishandled data​ | Automated Privacy Notice, Risk-Based Pricing & Adverse Action Fulfillment​ | Proves consumer disclosure compliance and reduces liability​
Title Washing & VIN Scams​ | Accepting falsified vehicle titles or buyer info​ | DL Scan Validation + AI Matching​ | Verifies documents + flags inconsistencies for desking managers​
Dead Deal Documentation Issues​ | Lost revenue, failed audits, no re-marketing value​ | Digital Dead Deal Jacket Storage + Reporting​ | Revives opportunities, improves compliance posture​
Consumer Trust & Reputation Damage​ | Bad reviews, chargebacks, and lender risk​ | CreditDriver Soft Pull with Consent Flow​ | Builds trust by offering consumer-first, score-safe prequal experience​

Informativ Objection Handling Playbook
Objection Blocking: Establishing Expertise Before Resistance
Topic: Compliance
Language:
A lot of dealers think they’re covered — until a lender or auditor asks for documentation, or a deal gets kicked back. That’s why we built Informativ to capture privacy notices, ID scans, and Red Flag steps digitally — so there’s never a question of whether you followed the right process.
Topic: Cost
Language:
Most of our dealers see Informativ pay for itself within the first few months — whether it’s from catching a single fraud case, converting more leads with soft pulls, or just saving on bureau fees. We designed it to deliver measurable ROI.
Topic: Fraud
Language:
Fraud is getting smarter — we’re seeing more synthetic IDs and mobile-first scams than ever before. Informativ uses multiple data points — like SIM validation, driver’s license scans, and soft pulls — to stop fraud before it starts.
Topic: Process Integration
Language:
Sales teams don’t want more tools. So Informativ was built to work with what they already do — scanning a license, pulling credit, and moving through the sale. Everything’s guided, intuitive, and fast.
Topic: Dealer Size
Language:
Whether you’re a single rooftop or a 50-store group, compliance, fraud, and efficiency matter. Informativ scales up or down — you get enterprise-level protection without needing enterprise-level staff.
Objection Handling Scenarios
Objection: “We just want basic credit pulls — why upgrade from Foundation?”
Tier: Detect / Protect / Control
Customer Type: Dealer that doesn’t value compliance or fraud protection; potentially unaware of risk.
Response:
I completely understand — and if credit is all you need, Foundation is a solid start.
However, most dealers see value in moving to Detect as a baseline for fraud and credit-related
compliance. Detect gives you that foundational protection.
As your operation grows, you become a bigger target for fraud — and with more deals and staff turnover, compliance concerns grow too. That’s where Protect comes in. With license scanning, lead push, and layered fraud detection, you’ll prevent deals from falling apart late in F&I.
Control goes even further — automating your lead-to-deal flow and qualifying customers with soft pulls before the desk ever touches it. It’s designed for fast-growing dealers who are starting to feel friction.
Follow-Up Questions:
“How much do recontracts cost you each month?”
“How is the quality of the leads you’re getting today?”
“If we could speed up your process to help you sell more cars while preventing fraud and ensuring compliance, would that be of interest?”
Objection: “Why would I pay for Detect if I’m not getting compliance tools?”
Tier: Protect
Customer Type: Credit-only users with growing fraud concerns.
Response:
Detect is about fraud defense at the front end and credit-related compliance — not full compliance.
With Protect, we step it up so you get:
License verification
Red Flags, OFAC, Out-of-Wallet questions
Synthetic ID detection
Hard stops before bad deals make it to F&I
If audit trails and workflow enforcement are your priority, then Protect or Control are better fits.
Follow-Up Question:
“Would stopping just one fraudulent deal this year be worth the upgrade?”
Objection: “We already use soft pulls elsewhere — why use Informativ’s?”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Franchise groups using a third-party soft pull vendor.
Response:
That’s a great question — and not all soft pulls are equal. Informativ’s soft pulls are mirror-image, delivering:
The same data as a hard pull (FICO, tradelines, risk metrics)
No credit impact or compliance burden
Direct API connection for credit application pre-fill
You also get this fully integrated into your workflow through our Compliance System — fewer keystrokes, more deals desked faster.
Follow-Up Question:
“Are your current soft pulls populating the credit app, or are reps still typing in customer information?”
Objection: “Why is Control so much more expensive?”
Tier: Control
Customer Type: Multi-rooftop dealer groups or high-volume franchises.
Response:
Control isn’t just another feature add-on — it’s a full operational enablement platform. You’re getting:
Customer Insights Dashboard — your marketing team and sales managers will love this
STIP request automation and secure document collection
Full compliance dashboards and digital audit trails
Think of Control as:
Fraud prevention + Workflow control + Deal funding acceleration — all in one bundle.
Follow-Up Question:
“What’s the cost of delayed funding or missing STIPs across your rooftops?”
Objection: “Can’t I downgrade tiers and just add the 1–2 things I need?”
Tier: Any
Customer Type: Value shopper
Response:
We hear that often — and here’s what we’ve learned:
Bundling is more than pricing — it’s operational design. The features within each tier are chosen to solve problems that appear together. Piecemealing often leaves gaps, delays ROI, or leads to avoidable risk.
We want your team to have everything they need to move quickly and confidently — not patch a solution later.
Follow-Up Question:
“If you add just one feature now, what gaps might be left in the process?”
Objection: “We already have fraud tools built into our CRM/DMS.”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Franchise dealers relying on native CRM/DMS capabilities.
Response:
That’s a common setup — but CRMs and DMSs aren’t built to protect you from fraud or enforce
proactive showroom compliance.
Informativ gives you:
Real-time fraud alerts (including synthetic ID detection)
Driver’s license scan with Red Flag validation
Showroom hard stops when fraud is detected
Guardrails for your team so you know from lead to sale the deal is compliant
Your CRM may show the lead — Informativ ensures it’s a safe, real one.
Follow-Up Question:
“What happens today if a fake ID gets through and lands in a deal jacket?”
Objection: “This sounds like more process for my team — we’re already swamped.”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Any growth-stage dealership
Response:
Totally fair. But our goal is to reduce friction — not add it.
eZ Form Fill and Customer Insights streamline the prequalification process
Soft pulls pre-qualify leads without effort
Enforced workflows automate decision points and reduce training burden
This isn’t more process — it’s fewer errors, less training, and faster closings.
Follow-Up Question:
“What would it mean to save 10 minutes per deal without sacrificing compliance?”

Informativ Persona-Based Discovery Guide
Use this guide during discovery to tailor your questions to each dealership persona. Align tiered offerings with their role-specific pain points and business objectives.
Dealer Principal / CEO / GM
Role: Oversees all ops; profit/loss accountability; often founder or managing partner
Pain Points:
High sensitivity to costs and vendor sprawl
Worries about legal risk or compliance failures
Protects brand and personal reputation
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently ensure compliance is enforced at all rooftops?
What happens when deals get recontracted due to income or ID issues?
How do you track fraud across rooftops today?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Accelerate (multi-rooftop oversight)
Red Flags: Mentions “I don’t want to change much” → push for Foundation/Control first
CFO / Controller / Comptroller
Role: Owns financial systems, DMS oversight, budgeting, cost containment
Pain Points:
Manual compliance costs or risks
Lack of standardized credit/fraud workflow across locations
Budget predictability
Discovery Questions:
What’s your current credit report spend across rooftops?
How do you handle income verification and STIPs tracking today?
Do you have monthly variance in F&I funding due to recontracts?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline or Accelerate (depending on size)
Red Flags: Already uses multiple vendors for credit — look for consolidation trigger
COO / Variable Ops / Platform Manager
Role: Workflow efficiency across sales and F&I; tool rollouts
Pain Points:
Sales team resistance to new processes
Lead leakage or duplication
Compliance gaps from user error
Discovery Questions:
How do you ensure every rep follows the same process?
What’s the delay between lead capture and deal start?
How are Red Flags or OFAC handled during your current sales flow?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline (for operational enforcement), Accelerate (for ROI ops)
Red Flags: If they call tech “hard to roll out,” show CONTROL → STREAMLINE path
General Sales Manager (GSM)
Role: Oversees new/used sales, team hiring, volume/gross targets
Pain Points:
Slower deals due to manual entry
Missed sales from bad leads or fraud
Sales reps skipping steps
Discovery Questions:
How do you know reps are following your process?
How often are deals delayed because of ID or app issues?
Do you use soft pulls to help reps prequalify on the floor?
Ideal Tier Alignment: CONTROL or STREAMLINE
Red Flags: Says “compliance slows us down” → use speed/fraud angle
Compliance Officer / Office Manager / F&I Director
Role: Ensures Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action; audit protection
Pain Points:
Paper-based systems or manual audits
F&I recontracts due to bad data
No way to prove compliance across rooftops
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently manage Red Flags and MLA checks?
Can you prove compliance in an audit today?
What’s your recontract rate due to missing income proofs?
Ideal Tier Alignment: FOUNDATION (for basics), STREAMLINE/ACCELERATE (for automation)
Red Flags: If they say “we have it covered,” ask *how they document it*

Features, Advantages, and Benefits of Informativ
1. TotalSolution Platform
Feature: Unified platform for credit, compliance, ID verification, and lead engagement.
Advantage: Streamlines processes by reducing the number of vendors and logins.
Benefit: Saves time and money while improving operational efficiency across F&I, Sales, and Compliance teams.
2. Credit Report Integration (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Feature: Pull hard and soft credit reports from major bureaus.
Advantage: Full flexibility depending on buyer engagement stage.
Benefit: Improve conversion with soft pulls during lead gen, and streamline funding with reliable hard pull accuracy.
3. Compliance Automation (Dealer Safeguard)
Feature: Automated compliance workflows (Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action, Privacy Notices).
Advantage: Ensures regulatory alignment without manual tracking.
Benefit: Reduces legal risk and audit exposure while protecting your dealership’s reputation.
4. Digital Deal Jacket Export for DMS Integration
Feature: Drag and drop deal jackets directly to DMS (2-step process).
Advantage: Ensures critical documents are securely and consistently archived.
Benefit: Simplifies compliance documentation and supports faster funding and auditing processes.
5. Mobile Driver’s License Scanning
Feature: Scan and verify IDs from any mobile device.
Advantage: Speeds up customer intake and ensures accurate data capture.
Benefit: Reduces fraud risk and enhances customer satisfaction during the initial interaction.
6. Website Credit Application Integration
Feature: Plug-and-play soft pull tools and credit applications for your dealership website.
Advantage: Instant consumer pre-qualification online.
Benefit: Increases lead quality and showroom traffic with pre-qualified buyers.
7. QR Code Lead Capture + Sales Enablement
Feature: Generate QR codes for vehicle listings, marketing, or on-lot displays.
Advantage: Creates an easy entry point for buyers and links directly to pre-qualification tools.
Benefit: Boosts lead capture and enables quicker buyer engagement, even after hours.
8. Real-Time Deal Alerts + Compliance Tracking Dashboard
Feature: Centralized dashboard to monitor deal activity and compliance flags.
Advantage: Gives managers live oversight of all open deals and alerts.
Benefit: Prevents deal delays and enhances accountability across the team.
9. Multi-Channel Support & Dedicated Account Management
Feature: White-glove onboarding and dealer-first support team.
Advantage: Personalized service and fast resolution times.
Benefit: Minimizes downtime and ensures a smooth transition from other providers.

Battle Card: Dealerships That Just Want Credit
Customer Profile & Needs
Typically 1 rooftop, limited F&I team
Price-sensitive and process-light
Needs credit access with minimal complexity
Recommended Tier: FOUNDATION
Tagline: Credit Reports Wherever You Need Them
Ideal for credit-only users who want reliable performance without compliance extras.
Key Product Benefits
Flat-Rate Credit Pricing
Refillable Credit Bank
Website Application (Prequal/Full App)
Credit Pull from RouteOne, DealerTrack, etc.

“FOUNDATION gives you simple, dependable access to credit reports from the platforms you already use—with no surprises and no extras you don’t need.”
Objection Handling Prompts
Competitive Differentiation

Objection | Response
“We’re small—we don’t need all that compliance stuff.” | Exactly. FOUNDATION delivers just the credit access you need, at a price that makes sense.
“We only use RouteOne.” | Perfect—FOUNDATION supports that integration natively. Pull credit without changing a thing.


Informativ FOUNDATION | Experian/Equifax Direct

Integration into RouteOne & DealerTrack | May require third-party bridge
Built-in prequal web app | Often unavailable

Battle Card: Cross-Sell / Upsell Opportunity
Customer Profile & Needs
Existing Informativ client on lower tier (Foundation or Detect)
Stable usage, open to improving efficiency or compliance
May not realize what higher tiers unlock
Upgrade Targets:
From FOUNDATION → DETECT or PROTECT
From DETECT → PROTECT or CONTROL
From PROTECT → CONTROL
💡 Key Product Benefits by Move

“You’re already seeing the value of Informativ—upgrading unlocks automation, insight, and efficiency that pays off in faster deals, fewer chargebacks, and stronger compliance.”
Objection Handling Prompts

Upgrade Path | Key Benefit
Foundation → Detect | Add compliance & basic fraud protection
Detect → Protect | Automate fraud alerts, enforce process steps, go paperless
Protect → Control | Optimize spend, speed up deal desk, gain customer insights


Objection | Response
“We’re good with what we have.” | That’s great—and this upgrade is about unlocking time and increasing sales. Let’s test it with one rooftop.
“Seems like overkill.” | You already pay for credit—what if your spend worked harder? CONTROL gives you ROI on every pull.

Battle Card: Large Franchise Dealer
Customer Profile & Needs
High-volume, multi-rooftop operations
Established F&I and compliance teams
Remote sales and digital retailing channels in use
Prioritize: efficiency, fraud prevention, enforceable workflows, integration across rooftops
Recommended Tier: CONTROL
Tagline: Optimized and Controlled Credit Usage & Enhanced Customer Insights
CONTROL delivers complete showroom oversight and advanced tools to minimize friction, manage costs, and empower high-performance teams.

Showroom Credit Spend Controls: Optimize usage across rooftops with alerts and guardrails
Customer Insights Dashboard: Boost closing rates by identifying buyer potential early
Compliance Enforcements: Real-time fraud alerts, Red Flags hard stops, enforced workflows
Soft Pull Integration: Reduce compliance burden while increasing engagement

Value Messaging
“CONTROL gives your group-level management a crystal-clear view of every credit pull, every fraud trigger, every compliance step—across all rooftops. Scale smarter, close faster, and enforce the process you already trust.”
Objection Handling Prompts
Competitive Differentiation

Objection | Response
“We already have a fraud solution.” | Most systems stop at detection. Informativ CONTROL automates enforcement with real-time process stops and showroom credit optimization.
“Compliance isn’t a major issue right now.” | FTC requirements have changed—CONTROL ensures you’re protected today and audit-ready tomorrow.
“It’s too much for our group.” | CONTROL is modular and configurable—roll out to high-risk rooftops first and scale as needed.


Informativ CONTROL | 700Credit/NCC
Real-time enforced workflows | Manual alert systems
Multi-rooftop dashboard visibility | Rooftop-level view only
Integrated soft pull strategy | Often requires third-party tools
Fully bundled with CBC credit pricing | À la carte add-ons drive up cost

Compliance Risk & Opportunity Audit Template

# Dealership Information

Dealer Name: ____________________________________
Address: _______________________________________
City/State/Zip: _________________________________
Date of Audit: __________________________________
AE Name: _______________________________________

# Safeguard Rule Compliance Review

1. Has the dealership implemented a written information security program (WISP)? [ ] Yes [ ] No
2. Are physical, administrative, and technical safeguards documented and consistently enforced? [ ] Yes [ ] No
3. Are there tools or processes in place to prevent staff from using personal devices to capture sensitive data (e.g. photos of DL/SSNs)? [ ] Yes [ ] No
4. Does the dealership perform annual training on data protection and compliance? [ ] Yes [ ] No
5. Are dead deals and digital deal jackets securely stored for 5 years? [ ] Yes [ ] No

# FCRA Credit Compliance Review

1. Are Adverse Action Letters being issued for all declined or dead deals within 30 days? [ ] Yes [ ] No

THE TIERED PRODUCT STORY
1. FOUNDATION
Tagline: Credit Reports Wherever You Need Them
Who It’s For: Dealerships that only need straightforward credit pulls through their preferred platform (i.e. – RouteOne, Dealertrack)—no extra frills, but does include a website credit application for consumers to pre-qualify and/or complete a full credit application on dealer’s websites.
Value Message: Reliable, flat-rate credit reports with a flexible credit bank that scales with your dealership’s needs, giving you the freedom to pull credit where and when you want.
Key Features:
No-nonsense credit reports
Pull credit from any supported system
Flexible, refillable credit bank
Simple, flat-rate pricing
2. DETECT
Tagline: Credit Reports with Essential Compliance & Fraud Protection
Who It’s For: Dealers that don’t yet need showroom-wide compliance controls but want to reduce risk on remote and online deals with basic compliance and fraud safeguards.  Compliance offering to meet credit regulatory requirements.
Value Message: Affordable credit pricing combined with built-in credit based compliance and fraud prevention features, helping you protect your dealership and your customers without overcomplicating your process.
Key Features:
Red Flags Hard Stop
Credit compliance tools
Integrated fraud detection for credit pulls
Soft pull and/or hard pull credit reports
3. PROTECT
Tagline: Enhanced Compliance & Showroom Guardrails
Who It’s For: Dealerships ready to take control of their in-store and remote compliance processes with advanced fraud prevention, digital document management, and enforceable workflows customized to meet each dealership’s current process.
Value Message: Achieve a compliant, paperless sales process with robust proactive compliance enforcement and fraud protection, ensuring every deal—remote or in-person—follows a consistent, defensible process.
Key Features:
Paperless sales process with e-signatures
Digital deal jacket creation and secure storage
Enforceable compliant workflows with enforced process stops and real time notifications of potential violations
Fraud detection in both showroom and remote environments
4. CONTROL
Tagline: Optimized and Controlled Credit Usage & Enhanced Customer Insights
Who It’s For: Dealers seeking to manage credit spend, minimize compliance burdens, and gain actionable insights to sell more efficiently and effectively.
Value Message: Drive smarter sales and stronger margins by controlling credit spend, integrating soft pulls to reduce compliance obligations, and leveraging real-time customer insights to close more deals faster.
Key Features:
Showroom credit spend controls and optimization
Seamless soft pull workflows to reduce compliance burden
Eliminate customer friction and improve buying experience
Speed up the process at the desk
Customer Insights dashboard to identify buying potential and improve closing rates
Tech to minimize compliance friction while maximizing opportunity

External Threat / Regulation​ | Dealer Pain / Risk​ | Informativ Solution​ | Value Delivered​
FTC Safeguards Rule​ | Risk of $51,744 per violation due to missing ID verification, unsecured PII, or lack of audit trail​ | Informativ’s Compliance Suite, Digital Deal Jackets​ | Automates compliance steps, secures PII, provides audit logs & manager oversight​
FTC CARS Rule​ | Deceptive practices, unclear disclosures → regulatory penalties​ | Digital Credit Apps + Prequal via Customer Insights​ | Enforces transparency & tracks buyer consent/documentation​
Auto Loan Fraud Surge​ | Synthetic identities, fake documents → unrecoverable vehicle losses​ | Fraud Suite: ID Scan + Barcode Check, SIM/Device Verification, Soft Pull Fraud Insights​ | Stops fake identities before the deal, validates buyer authenticity​
CDK Data Breach​ | Operational shutdown, lost consumer data trust​ | Secure Document Collection Portal, PII Encryption & User Controls​ | Protects dealership from ransomware risk & enforces employee data handling security​
State-Level Privacy Laws​ | Exposure to consumer lawsuits over mishandled data​ | Automated Privacy Notice, Risk-Based Pricing & Adverse Action Fulfillment​ | Proves consumer disclosure compliance and reduces liability​
Title Washing & VIN Scams​ | Accepting falsified vehicle titles or buyer info​ | DL Scan Validation + AI Matching​ | Verifies documents + flags inconsistencies for desking managers​
Dead Deal Documentation Issues​ | Lost revenue, failed audits, no re-marketing value​ | Digital Dead Deal Jacket Storage + Reporting​ | Revives opportunities, improves compliance posture​
Consumer Trust & Reputation Damage​ | Bad reviews, chargebacks, and lender risk​ | CreditDriver Soft Pull with Consent Flow​ | Builds trust by offering consumer-first, score-safe prequal experience​

Informativ Objection Handling Playbook
Objection Blocking: Establishing Expertise Before Resistance
Topic: Compliance
Language: A lot of dealers think they’re covered — until a lender or auditor asks for documentation, or a deal gets kicked back. That’s why we built Informativ to capture privacy notices, ID scans, and Red Flag steps digitally — so there’s never a question of whether you followed the right process.
Topic: Cost
Language: Most of our dealers see Informativ pay for itself within the first few months — whether it’s from catching a single fraud case, converting more leads with soft pulls, or just saving on bureau fees. We designed it to deliver measurable ROI.
Topic: Fraud
Language: Fraud is getting smarter — we’re seeing more synthetic IDs and mobile-first scams than ever before. Informativ uses multiple data points — like SIM validation, DL scans, and soft pulls — to stop fraud before it starts.
Topic: Process Integration
Language: Sales teams don’t want more tools. So Informativ was built to work with what they already do — scanning a license, pulling credit, and moving through the sale. Everything’s guided, intuitive, and faster.
Topic: Dealer Size
Language: Whether you’re a single rooftop or a 50-store group, compliance, fraud, and efficiency matter. Informativ scales up or down — you get enterprise-level protection without needing enterprise-level staff.
Objection Handling
Objection: “We just want basic credit pulls — why upgrade from Foundation?”
Tier: Detect / Protect / Control
Customer Type: Dealer that doesn’t value compliance or fraud protection, potentially doesn’t understand the risk.
Response:
I completely understand — and if credit is all you need, Foundation is a solid start.
However, most dealers see value in moving to Detect as a starting point for baseline fraud and credit related compliance all in one place.  Detect gives you that baseline protection.  As your operation grows you become a bigger target for fraud and with more deals and staff turnovercompliance starts becoming a concern, that is where Protect can be a great option. With license scanning, lead push, and layered fraud detection, you’ll prevent deals from falling apart late in F&I.
Control goes further — automating your lead-to-deal flow and qualifying customers with soft pulls before the desk ever touches it. It’s designed for fast-growing dealers who are starting to feel friction.
Follow-Up Question:
“How much do recontracts cost you each month?”
“How are the quality of the leads you are getting today?”
“If we could speed up your process to help you sell more cars while preventing fraud and ensuring compliance, would that be of interest?”
Objection: “Why would I pay for Detect if I’m not getting compliance tools?”
Tier: Protect
Customer Type: Credit-only users with growing fraud concerns
Response: Detect is about fraud defense at the front-end and credit related compliance, not full compliance. With Protect we step that up so you would be getting:
License verification
Red Flags, OFAC, Out-Of-Wallet Questions
Synthetic ID detection
Hard stops before bad deals make it to F&I
If audit trails and workflow enforcement are your priority, then Protect or Control are better fits.
Follow-Up Question: “Would stopping just one fraudulent deal this year be worth the upgrade?”
Objection: “We already use soft pulls elsewhere — why use Informativ’s?”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Franchise groups using a third-party soft pull vendor
Response: That’s a great question — and not all soft pulls are equal. Informativ’s soft pulls are mirror-image, delivering:
The same data as a hard pull (FICO, tradelines, risk metrics)
No credit impact or compliance burden
Direct API connection for credit application pre-fill
You also get this integrated into your workflow through our Compliance System — fewer keystrokes, more deals desked faster.
Follow-Up Question: “Are your current soft pulls populating the credit app, or are reps still typing in customer information?”
Objection: “Why is Control so much more expensive?”
Tier: Control
Customer Type: Multi-rooftop dealer groups or high-volume franchise
Response: Control isn’t just another feature add-on — it’s a full operational enablement platform.
You’re getting:
Customer Insights Dashboard – your marketing team and sales managers will love this.
STIP request automation and secure document collection
Full compliance dashboards and digital audit trails
Think of Control as:
Fraud prevention + Workflow control + Deal funding acceleration — all in one bundle.
Follow-Up Question: “What’s the cost of delayed funding or missing STIPs across your rooftops?”
Objection: “Can’t I downgrade tiers and just add the 1-2 things I need?”
Tier: Any
Customer Type: Value shopper
Response:  Here’s what we’ve learned:
Bundling is more than pricing — it’s operational design. The features within each tier are chosen to solve problems that appear together. Piecemealing often leaves gaps, delays ROI, or leads to avoidable risk.
We want your team to have everything they need to move quickly and confidently, not patch a solution later.
Follow-Up Question:
“If you add just one feature now, what gaps might be left in the process?”
Objection: “We already have fraud tools built into our CRM/DMS.”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Franchise dealer relying on native CRM/DMS capabilities
Response: That’s a common setup — but CRMs and DMSs are not built to protect you from fraud or enforce proactive showroom compliance.
Informativ gives you:
Real-time fraud alerts (including synthetic ID detection)
Driver’s license scan with Red Flag validation
Showroom hard stops when fraud is detected
Guardrails for your team so you know from lead to sale the deal is compliant
Your CRM may show the lead — Informativ ensures it’s a safe, real one.
Follow-Up Question: “What happens today if a fake ID gets through and lands in a deal jacket?”
Objection: “This sounds like more process for my team — we’re already swamped.”
Tier: Protect / Control
Customer Type: Any growth-stage dealership
Response: Totally fair. But our solution reduces friction, not add it.
eZ Form Fill and Customer Insights streamlines the prequalification process
Soft pulls pre-qualify leads without effort
Enforced workflows automate decision points and reduce training burden
This isn’t more process — it’s fewer errors, less training, and faster closings.
Follow-Up Question:
“What would it mean to save 10 minutes per deal without sacrificing compliance?”

Informativ Persona-Based Discovery Guide
Use this guide during discovery to tailor your questions to each dealership persona. Align tiered offerings with their role-specific pain points and business objectives.
Dealer Principal / CEO / GM
Role: Oversees all ops; profit/loss accountability; often founder or managing partner
Pain Points:
High sensitivity to costs and vendor sprawl
Worries about legal risk or compliance failures
Protects brand and personal reputation
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently ensure compliance is enforced at all rooftops?
What happens when deals get recontracted due to income or ID issues?
How do you track fraud across rooftops today?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Accelerate (multi-rooftop oversight)
Red Flags: Mentions “I don’t want to change much” → push for Foundation/Control first
CFO / Controller / Comptroller
Role: Owns financial systems, DMS oversight, budgeting, cost containment
Pain Points:
Manual compliance costs or risks
Lack of standardized credit/fraud workflow across locations
Budget predictability
Discovery Questions:
What’s your current credit report spend across rooftops?
How do you handle income verification and STIPs tracking today?
Do you have monthly variance in F&I funding due to recontracts?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline or Accelerate (depending on size)
Red Flags: Already uses multiple vendors for credit — look for consolidation trigger
COO / Variable Ops / Platform Manager
Role: Workflow efficiency across sales and F&I; tool rollouts
Pain Points:
Sales team resistance to new processes
Lead leakage or duplication
Compliance gaps from user error
Discovery Questions:
How do you ensure every rep follows the same process?
What’s the delay between lead capture and deal start?
How are Red Flags or OFAC handled during your current sales flow?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline (for operational enforcement), Accelerate (for ROI ops)
Red Flags: If they call tech “hard to roll out,” show CONTROL → STREAMLINE path
General Sales Manager (GSM)
Role: Oversees new/used sales, team hiring, volume/gross targets
Pain Points:
Slower deals due to manual entry
Missed sales from bad leads or fraud
Sales reps skipping steps
Discovery Questions:
How do you know reps are following your process?
How often are deals delayed because of ID or app issues?
Do you use soft pulls to help reps prequalify on the floor?
Ideal Tier Alignment: CONTROL or STREAMLINE
Red Flags: Says “compliance slows us down” → use speed/fraud angle
Compliance Officer / Office Manager / F&I Director
Role: Ensures Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action; audit protection
Pain Points:
Paper-based systems or manual audits
F&I recontracts due to bad data
No way to prove compliance across rooftops
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently manage Red Flags and MLA checks?
Can you prove compliance in an audit today?
What’s your recontract rate due to missing income proofs?
Ideal Tier Alignment: FOUNDATION (for basics), STREAMLINE/ACCELERATE (for automation)
Red Flags: If they say “we have it covered,” ask *how they document it*

Features, Advantages, and Benefits of Informativ
1. TotalSolution Platform
Feature: Unified platform for credit, compliance, ID verification, and lead engagement.
Advantage: Streamlines processes by reducing the number of vendors and logins.
Benefit: Saves time and money while improving operational efficiency across F&I, Sales, and Compliance teams.
2. Credit Report Integration (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Feature: Pull hard and soft credit reports from major bureaus.
Advantage: Full flexibility depending on buyer engagement stage.
Benefit: Improve conversion with soft pulls during lead gen, and streamline funding with reliable hard pull accuracy.
3. Compliance Automation (Dealer Safeguard)
Feature: Automated compliance workflows (Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action, Privacy Notices).
Advantage: Ensures regulatory alignment without manual tracking.
Benefit: Reduces legal risk and audit exposure while protecting your dealership’s reputation.
4. Digital Deal Jacket Export for DMS Integration
Feature: Drag and drop deal jackets directly to DMS (2-step process).
Advantage: Ensures critical documents are securely and consistently archived.
Benefit: Simplifies compliance documentation and supports faster funding and auditing processes.
5. Mobile Driver’s License Scanning
Feature: Scan and verify IDs from any mobile device.
Advantage: Speeds up customer intake and ensures accurate data capture.
Benefit: Reduces fraud risk and enhances customer satisfaction during the initial interaction.
6. Website Credit Application Integration
Feature: Plug-and-play soft pull tools and credit applications for your dealership website.
Advantage: Instant consumer pre-qualification online.
Benefit: Increases lead quality and showroom traffic with pre-qualified buyers.
7. QR Code Lead Capture + Sales Enablement
Feature: Generate QR codes for vehicle listings, marketing, or on-lot displays.
Advantage: Creates an easy entry point for buyers and links directly to pre-qualification tools.
Benefit: Boosts lead capture and enables quicker buyer engagement, even after hours.
8. Real-Time Deal Alerts + Compliance Tracking Dashboard
Feature: Centralized dashboard to monitor deal activity and compliance flags.
Advantage: Gives managers live oversight of all open deals and alerts.
Benefit: Prevents deal delays and enhances accountability across the team.
9. Multi-Channel Support & Dedicated Account Management
Feature: White-glove onboarding and dealer-first support team.
Advantage: Personalized service and fast resolution times.
Benefit: Minimizes downtime and ensures a smooth transition from other providers.

Prospecting
Objection: New product/upgrade to TS will bring changes to internal processes.
Rebuttal:
Totally fair — but the goal isn’t to add more process, it’s to remove the most time-wasting, repetitive steps. Informativ automates compliance, reduces rework, and makes the process consistent across reps. We replace manual friction with guided, faster workflows.
Objection: Compliance doesn’t matter now that the CARS rule is dead.
Rebuttal:
CARS rule aside, the FTC Safeguards Rule is very much alive — and it’s enforceable. Beyond regulation, compliance is how you protect your business from lawsuits, chargebacks, and public exposure.
Objection: We’ve never experienced fraud.
Rebuttal:
That’s great — but fraud is growing fast. Synthetic IDs, mobile-first scams, and social engineering are harder to detect. Informativ puts controls in place before you become a target.
Objection: Not adding any new expenses. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
What if we could lower your overall cost by replacing 2–3 separate vendors with one? Informativ isn’t just a new expense — it’s a smarter investment with real ROI.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
Discovery
Objection: I want the flexibility to run as many credit pulls as I want.
Rebuttal:
That flexibility comes at a cost — literally. Most dealers overspend on unnecessary pulls that don’t fund. We help you qualify customers smarter, reduce cost per deal, and still give you override options when needed.
Objection: I don’t want to change my process.

Title Washing & VIN Scams | Accepting falsified vehicle titles or buyer info | DL Scan Validation + AI Matching | Verifies documents + flags inconsistencies for desking managers
Dead Deal Documentation Issues | Lost revenue, failed audits, no re-marketing value | Digital Dead Deal Jacket Storage + Reporting | Revives opportunities, improves compliance posture
Consumer Trust & Reputation Damage | Bad reviews, chargebacks, and lender risk | CreditDriver Soft Pull with Consent Flow | Builds trust by offering consumer-first, score-safe prequal experience

# Informativ Persona-Based Discovery Guide

Use this guide during discovery to tailor your questions to each dealership persona. Align tiered offerings with their role-specific pain points and business objectives.

## Dealer Principal / CEO / GM

Role: Oversees all ops; profit/loss accountability; often founder or managing partner
Pain Points:
High sensitivity to costs and vendor sprawl
Worries about legal risk or compliance failures
Protects brand and personal reputation
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently ensure compliance is enforced at all rooftops?
What happens when deals get recontracted due to income or ID issues?
How do you track fraud across rooftops today?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Accelerate (multi-rooftop oversight)
Red Flags: Mentions “I don’t want to change much” → push for Foundation/Control first
⸻

## CFO / Controller / Comptroller

Role: Owns financial systems, DMS oversight, budgeting, cost containment
Pain Points:
Manual compliance costs or risks
Lack of standardized credit/fraud workflow across locations
Budget predictability
Discovery Questions:
What’s your current credit report spend across rooftops?
How do you handle income verification and STIPs tracking today?
Do you have monthly variance in F&I funding due to recontracts?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline or Accelerate (depending on size)
Red Flags: Already uses multiple vendors for credit — look for consolidation trigger
⸻

## COO / Variable Ops / Platform Manager

Role: Workflow efficiency across sales and F&I; tool rollouts
Pain Points:
Sales team resistance to new processes
Lead leakage or duplication
Compliance gaps from user error
Discovery Questions:
How do you ensure every rep follows the same process?
What’s the delay between lead capture and deal start?
How are Red Flags or OFAC handled during your current sales flow?
Ideal Tier Alignment: Streamline (for operational enforcement), Accelerate (for ROI ops)
Red Flags: If they call tech “hard to roll out,” show CONTROL → STREAMLINE path
⸻

## General Sales Manager (GSM)

Role: Oversees new/used sales, team hiring, volume/gross targets
Pain Points:
Slower deals due to manual entry
Missed sales from bad leads or fraud
Sales reps skipping steps
Discovery Questions:
How do you know reps are following your process?
How often are deals delayed because of ID or app issues?
Do you use soft pulls to help reps prequalify on the floor?
Ideal Tier Alignment: CONTROL or STREAMLINE
Red Flags: Says “compliance slows us down” → use speed/fraud angle
⸻

## Compliance Officer / Office Manager / F&I Director

Role: Ensures Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action; audit protection
Pain Points:
Paper-based systems or manual audits
F&I recontracts due to bad data
No way to prove compliance across rooftops
Discovery Questions:
How do you currently manage Red Flags and MLA checks?
Can you prove compliance in an audit today?
What’s your recontract rate due to missing income proofs?
Ideal Tier Alignment: FOUNDATION (for basics), STREAMLINE/ACCELERATE (for automation)
Red Flags: If they say “we have it covered,” ask *how they document it*
⸻

# Informativ Quote Confidence Checklist (Tiered Packages)

Use this checklist before finalizing any quote. Ensure you're aligning value with buyer pain, selecting the correct tier, and preparing the deal for smooth close and post-sale success.

## 1. Customer Discovery Alignment

☐ Discovery notes confirm correct tier (Foundation, Control, Streamline, Accelerate)
☐ Customer pain points are well-documented (fraud, compliance, speed, funding delays)
☐ Buyer persona is identified (GM, F&I, Desk, Compliance Officer)
☐ Competitive options were surfaced and value-differentiated

## 2. Tier Fit & Value Match

☐ Selected package matches the business pain — no tier over/under-selling
☐ Tier features were reviewed and explained using Battle Cards or Proposal Slides
☐ Customer acknowledged understanding of what’s included and not included
☐ Tier aligns with buyer type (e.g., Accelerate for enterprise/multi-rooftop)

## 3. Objections & Pricing Strategy

☐ Any discount was justified with a value-based tradeoff (e.g., fewer STIPs, limited soft pulls)
☐ Buyer agreed to token expectations or monthly pricing structure
☐ Rebuttals were documented using the Objection Handling Playbook
☐ No unnecessary downgrading occurred to “close quickly”

## 4. Proposal & CPQ Accuracy

☐ Correct SKUs selected for tier in CPQ
☐ Pricing tiers and volume levels match CPQ configuration
☐ All proposal slides or inserts tailored to selected tier are attached
☐ Deal is approved if discount > approved threshold

## 5. Internal Handoff Ready

☐ CS/Implementation has access to all relevant tier details and pain points
☐ Sales has recorded post-sale expectations (e.g., income verification, STIPs)
☐ Customer understands next steps tied to their tier (e.g., Accelerate training or dashboard access)

Audience: Regional Sales Managers

Compliance Risk Mitigation
“How much would a failed audit or missed Adverse Action notice cost you?”
“We’re not just a software tool—we’re your legal and reputational safeguard.”
Vendor Consolidation
“You’re using 3–5 tools where we offer one platform with fewer logins, fewer invoices, and greater accountability.”
“Bundling your credit, compliance, and CRM integration gives you centralized control and group-wide visibility.”
Time & Workflow ROI
“What is 30 minutes saved per deal worth across all rooftops monthly?”
“Faster deal jacket processing = more time selling and less time chasing paperwork.”
Audit & FTC Safeguards Rule Readiness
“If the FTC audited you today, would every rooftop be covered?”
“Informativ doesn’t just digitize your paperwork—it bulletproofs your process.”

CEO / Dealer / GM
CFO / Controller / Comptroller
COO

Dealer Principal / CEO
Close Hook: “Let’s run a 90-day trial and measure how much time and liability we eliminate.”
Incentive Strategy: Offer bundled discount or bonus month if signed by end of quarter.
Decision Bias: Ego-aligned positioning (e.g., “Best-in-class dealers like yours...”).
CFO / Comptroller
Close Hook: “Let’s schedule a cost analysis side-by-side with your current stack.”
Incentive Strategy: Tiered volume pricing by rooftop or rooftop+credit pull discount combo.
Decision Bias: Data, documentation, detailed ROI analysis.
COO
Close Hook: “If I show you how this standardizes workflows without disrupting stores, are you open to onboarding this quarter?”
Incentive Strategy: ROI case study with group-wide metrics + implementation SLA commitment.
Decision Bias: Operational impact, system reliability, group-level control.
Negotiation Cheat Sheet

Persona | Motivations | Price Sensitivity | Buying Behavior
Dealer Principal / GM / CEO | Maximize profitability, reduce liability, safeguard brand | High on commoditized items; negotiates emotionally | Prefers transparency, avoids vendor overload, resists change
CFO / Comptroller / Controller | Financial oversight, risk mitigation, cost control | Very High; expects itemized justification | Needs detailed pricing and ROI proof, dislikes vague “bundles”
COO | Process consistency, rollout simplicity, standardization | Moderate; values quality for strategic fit | Informed, operationally focused, strategic, SME in implementation


Objection | Response
“This feels expensive.” | “Compared to what you’d pay in legal exposure, or time lost due to compliance errors, this is a safeguard—not a splurge.”
“Can we do a pilot before committing?” | “We offer flexible monthly terms to prove the value without locking you in. You’ll see ROI by deal jacket count alone.”
“We already have a tool for that.” | “And we often replace 2–3 tools with one platform. Let’s map your stack together to identify overlap.”


Objection | Response
“Need to see cost breakdown.” | “Absolutely. I’ll provide a line-item quote showing soft pull volume, per-store compliance cost, and bundled savings.”
“Is this a fixed or variable cost?” | “We offer fixed-rate and usage-based models. You can choose what aligns best with your forecast.”
“We already budgeted with current vendors.” | “Let’s model a transition timeline—many of our partners recoup switching costs in 30–60 days through reduced risk and labor savings.”


Objection | Response
“How disruptive is implementation?” | “It’s plug-and-play with your DMS and CRM. Minimal lift for store managers. We’ve done this at 1-rooftop independents and 40-store groups.”
“Is this scalable across rooftops?” | “Yes—and our dashboard lets you monitor compliance activity group-wide in real time.”
“We’re mid-rollout with another vendor.” | “Happy to collaborate. We’ve replaced other systems mid-rollout with no data loss or operational downtime.”


Tactic | When to Use | How to Frame
Bundled Discount | Mult rooftop or multi-product deal | “Let’s bundle compliance, credit, and CRM feeds to reduce your effective cost by X%.”
Pilot-to-Full Model | Hesitant buyer, mid-year budget | “Start with 1–2 rooftops on a 60-day test and scale once results validate.”
Risk Aversion Close | Post-FTC scare or audit | “Let’s prevent one lawsuit or fine from derailing your year.”
Cost Justification Worksheet | CFO-led deals | “Let’s walk through a deal-by-deal cost-saving analysis with soft pulls, time savings, and liability exposure.”

Informativ Value Selling Training Program
Empower Reps to Sell with Confidence, Strategy, and Proof
I. INTRODUCTION: Selling the Way Dealers Buy
A. Training Purpose
To help reps master value-based selling by connecting Informativ’s Total Solution to the pain points, risks, and financial goals of dealerships.
B. Informativ’s Sales Philosophy – “E3”
Empathize with the dealership’s challenges
Educate them on the market and our solutions
Empower them to make a confident, defensible decision
II. THE VALUE MINDSET
A. What Is Value Selling?
Selling outcomes — not features. Aligning with dealer concerns:
Compliance Risk
Fraud Exposure
Sales Inefficiency
Credit Costs
B. 3 Primary Value Drivers:
III. PERSONA-BASED DISCOVERY & TIER MAPPING
Use the Persona Discovery Guide to match problems to packages .
IV. THE TIERED PRODUCT STORY
1. FOUNDATION
Basic Compliance Credit Pulls

2. CONTROL

3. STREAMLINE
Compliance & Sales Automation

4. ACCELERATE
Multi-Rooftop Control & Funding Speed

Key Features: Complete Fraud Suite, Compliance Guardrails, Customer Insights
V. PROOF POINTS & ROI FRAMEWORK

VI. OBJECTION HANDLING BY PACKAGE

VIII. LIVE PRACTICE SCENARIOS (Optional Coaching Modules)

IX. SALES PROCESS STAGES + ENABLEMENT TOOLS
A rep’s playbook for executing the Informativ Value Sell, from prospect to post-sale handoff.
X. WRAP-UP & REINFORCEMENT

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — Core Value Propositions and Talk Tracks
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — Core Value Propositions and Talk Tracks

VALUE PROP 1 — Move from Estimated to Qualified Payments:
Most platforms still show "estimated" payments that fall apart in-store. Informativ delivers real, credit-qualified, transactable payments, calculated exactly the way a dealership desks a deal. This closes the expectation gap that kills trust and conversion.

VALUE PROP 2 — More Than Penny-Perfect… We Return the Best Payment:
Legacy systems produce penny-perfect math. Informativ optimizes for the best payment — one that blends incentives, lender programs, and dealer margin to produce lower payments for shoppers and higher gross for dealers. No one else aligns both sides.

VALUE PROP 3 — Built for Software Companies, Not Legacy Dealer Tools:
Where competitors retrofitted old desking systems into APIs, Informativ Payments is cloud-native from day one. Low latency, modern architecture, and clean documentation make integration fast and future-proof for product teams.

VALUE PROP 4 — Usage-Based Pricing Dealers Actually Love:
Partners only pay for the VINs they push payments to, not a blanket rooftop fee. This makes payment experiences more affordable to dealers and more flexible to package inside partner platforms.

VALUE PROP 5 — Compliance as a Core Capability:
Powered by Informativ's credit, identity, and compliance backbone used by thousands of dealers. Every payment meets lender, OEM, and regulatory requirements. Partners get an enterprise-ready layer of safety without added engineering or legal overhead.

VALUE PROP 6 — Payments Everywhere (Ads, Websites, Chat, CRM, Digital Retailing):
Informativ Payments plugs into every touchpoint — from lead forms and AI chat to ad platforms and online desking. Partners can show accurate, credit-qualified payments across the entire shopper journey.

VALUE PROP 7 — Speed, Freshness, and Reliability:
Engine updates incentives, taxes, lender programs, and dealer rules in near real-time and responds in milliseconds. No stale data. No caching delays. No performance drag — just clean, live, fundable numbers.

THE OUTCOME:
A modern, API-driven payment experience that builds trust with shoppers, raises dealer profitability, improves conversion, reduces surprises and re-desks, and differentiates the partner platform instantly. Informativ Payments turns affordability into a competitive advantage.

Objections From Team Survey
